CWE-1265.detection_notes: Statically detectable via taint analysis, config parsing, handler inspection, and regex on dangerous patterns (e.g., .Raw( with + or fmt.Sprintf in query context).

CWE-1286.name: Improper Validation of Syntactic Correctness of Input
CWE-1286.description: The product receives input that is expected to be well-formed - i.e., to comply with a certain syntax - but it does not validate or incorrectly validates that the input complies with the syntax.
CWE-1286.original_description: The product receives input that is expected to be well-formed - i.e., to comply with a certain syntax - but it does not validate or incorrectly validates that the input complies with the syntax.
CWE-1286.detection_notes: Statically detectable via taint analysis, config parsing, handler inspection, and regex on dangerous patterns (e.g., .Raw( with + or fmt.Sprintf in query context).

CWE-1289.name: Improper Validation of Unsafe Equivalence in Input
CWE-1289.description: The product receives an input value that is used as a resource identifier or other type of reference, but it does not validate or incorrectly validates that the input is equivalent to a potentially-unsafe value.
CWE-1289.original_description: The product receives an input value that is used as a resource identifier or other type of reference, but it does not validate or incorrectly validates that the input is equivalent to a potentially-unsafe value.
CWE-1289.detection_notes: Statically detectable via taint analysis, config parsing, handler inspection, and regex on dangerous patterns (e.g., .Raw( with + or fmt.Sprintf in query context).

CWE-1322.name: Use of Blocking Code in Single-threaded, Non-blocking Context
CWE-1322.description: The product uses a non-blocking model that relies on a single threaded process for features such as scalability, but it contains code that can block when it is invoked.
CWE-1322.original_description: The product uses a non-blocking model that relies on a single threaded process for features such as scalability, but it contains code that can block when it is invoked.
CWE-1322.detection_notes: Statically detectable via taint analysis, config parsing, handler inspection, and regex on dangerous patterns (e.g., .Raw( with + or fmt.Sprintf in query context).

CWE-1327.name: Binding to an Unrestricted IP Address
CWE-1327.description: Binding to an unrestricted IP address. Very common Go mistake: calling http.ListenAndServe(":8080", router) or similar, which binds to 0.0.0.0 and exposes the service (including database ports) to the entire network.
CWE-1327.original_description: The product assigns the address 0.0.0.0 for a database server, a cloud service/instance, or any computing resource that communicates remotely.
CWE-1327.detection_notes: Statically detectable via taint analysis, config parsing, handler inspection, and regex on dangerous patterns (e.g., .Raw( with + or fmt.Sprintf in query context).

CWE-1333.name: Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity
CWE-1333.description: Inefficient regular expression complexity (ReDoS). Go's regexp package can suffer from catastrophic backtracking when evil regex patterns are used to validate or filter user input in Gin handlers or GORM query builders.
CWE-1333.original_description: The product uses a regular expression with a worst-case computational complexity that is inefficient and possibly exponential.
CWE-1333.detection_notes: Statically detectable via taint analysis, config parsing, handler inspection, and regex on dangerous patterns (e.g., .Raw( with + or fmt.Sprintf in query context).

CWE-1389.name: Incorrect Parsing of Numbers with Different Radices
CWE-1389.description: The product parses numeric input assuming base 10 (decimal) values, but it does not account for inputs that use a different base number (radix).
CWE-1389.original_description: The product parses numeric input assuming base 10 (decimal) values, but it does not account for inputs that use a different base number (radix).
CWE-1389.detection_notes: Statically detectable via taint analysis, config parsing, handler inspection, and regex on dangerous patterns (e.g., .Raw( with + or fmt.Sprintf in query context).

CWE-1392.name: Use of Default Credentials
CWE-1392.description: The product uses default credentials (such as passwords or cryptographic keys) for potentially critical functionality.
CWE-1392.original_description: The product uses default credentials (such as passwords or cryptographic keys) for potentially critical functionality.
CWE-1392.detection_notes: Statically detectable via taint analysis, config parsing, handler inspection, and regex on dangerous patterns (e.g., .Raw( with + or fmt.Sprintf in query context).

PERF-001.name: Regular Expression Compilation Inside Loop
PERF-001.description: Compiles a regular expression inside a Go loop body instead of compiling once and reusing the compiled pattern.
PERF-001.original_description: Detect regexp.MustCompile(...) or regexp.Compile(...) inside a for or range loop body.
PERF-001.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match regexp.MustCompile or regexp.Compile inside loop bodies and suppress when compilation is hoisted outside the loop.

PERF-002.name: String Concatenation Inside Loop
PERF-002.description: Repeatedly grows a string with concatenation inside a loop instead of using a builder, buffer, or batched join.
PERF-002.original_description: Detect s = s + part or s += part inside a for or range loop body.
PERF-002.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match repeated string concatenation in loops and suppress when strings.Builder, bytes.Buffer, or equivalent batching is already used.

PERF-003.name: Slice Rebuild Inside Loop
PERF-003.description: Rebuilds a working slice inside a loop in a way that repeatedly discards capacity or recreates the same accumulation buffer.
PERF-003.original_description: Detect loop-local slice re-creation followed by repeated append in the same loop body.
PERF-003.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match slice reset or make patterns inside loops and suppress when reuse via slicing or intentional one-shot allocation is evident.

PERF-004.name: Map Allocation Inside Loop
PERF-004.description: Allocates a new map inside a loop body where the map could be reused or hoisted to avoid repeated allocation churn.
PERF-004.original_description: Detect make(map[K]V) inside a for or range loop body.
PERF-004.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match make(map[...]) inside loop bodies and suppress when the allocation is intentional and not on a hot path.

PERF-005.name: JSON Encode Or Decode In Hot Loop
PERF-005.description: Performs repeated JSON marshal, unmarshal, encoder, or decoder setup inside an inner loop or row-by-row hot path.
PERF-005.original_description: Detect json.Marshal, json.Unmarshal, json.NewEncoder, or json.NewDecoder in a tight loop or repeated request-side hot path.
PERF-005.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match repeated JSON conversion in hot paths and suppress when the code already batches work or reuses encoder or decoder state appropriately.

PERF-006.name: fmt Formatting Inside Loop
PERF-006.description: Uses fmt-based string formatting repeatedly inside a loop where direct writes, builders, or cached formatting would be cheaper.
PERF-006.original_description: Detect fmt.Sprintf or fmt.Fprintf used as repeated string construction in a loop.
PERF-006.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match fmt formatting in loop bodies and suppress when the code is already writing directly to a reusable buffer or writer.

PERF-007.name: Defer Inside Loop
PERF-007.description: Places defer statements inside loops, causing cleanup work to stack until function exit and adding avoidable per-iteration overhead.
PERF-007.original_description: Detect defer inside a for or range loop body.
PERF-007.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match defer statements in loops and suppress when the loop is not on a hot path or when a helper function intentionally scopes the defer per iteration.

PERF-008.name: Time Parsing Inside Loop
PERF-008.description: Repeatedly parses time values in a loop using a constant layout on a hot path instead of restructuring or caching work.
PERF-008.original_description: Detect time.Parse or time.ParseInLocation inside loop bodies.
PERF-008.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match repeated time parsing inside loops and suppress when the parse is outside the hot path or the loop is clearly small and bounded.

PERF-009.name: URL Parsing Inside Loop
PERF-009.description: Repeatedly parses URLs inside a tight loop rather than moving parsing or validation to a cheaper boundary.
PERF-009.original_description: Detect url.Parse or url.ParseRequestURI inside loop bodies.
PERF-009.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match repeated URL parsing in loops and suppress when parsing happens on a cold path or only for a trivially small collection.

PERF-010.name: Template Parsing On Request Path
PERF-010.description: Parses templates on the request path instead of compiling them once at process start or initialization time.
PERF-010.original_description: Detect template.New(...).Parse(...), template.ParseFiles(...), or template.Must(template.Parse...) inside handlers or middleware.
PERF-010.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match template parsing on request-serving paths and suppress when template initialization is intentionally isolated and cached.

PERF-011.name: HTTP Client Allocation Per Request
PERF-011.description: Allocates a new http.Client on request-serving paths where a shared client should usually be reused.
PERF-011.original_description: Detect http.Client construction inside handlers, middleware, or looped call paths.
PERF-011.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match http.Client allocation on hot paths and suppress when a short-lived specialized client is intentionally required and not repeatedly recreated.

PERF-012.name: Prepared Statement Setup On Request Path
PERF-012.description: Creates prepared statements on request-serving paths instead of caching or initializing them once.
PERF-012.original_description: Detect db.Prepare or db.PrepareContext inside handlers, middleware, or inner loops.
PERF-012.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match prepared statement setup on hot paths and suppress when statement creation is amortized, cached, or intentionally scoped.

PERF-013.name: time.After Allocation In Long-Running Loop
PERF-013.description: Creates time.After values repeatedly inside long-running loops where reusable timers or tickers would avoid churn.
PERF-013.original_description: Detect time.After(...) inside worker loops, retry loops, or polling loops.
PERF-013.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match time.After in long-running loops and suppress when the loop is clearly bounded or already uses a reusable timer strategy.

PERF-014.name: Directory Scan Inside Loop
PERF-014.description: Performs repeated filesystem directory scans inside loops when the scanned set is stable and could be hoisted or cached.
PERF-014.original_description: Detect filepath.Glob, os.ReadDir, or similar directory scans inside loop bodies.
PERF-014.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match directory scans inside loops and suppress when the scan target is intentionally dynamic per iteration.

PERF-015.name: strconv Formatting Inside Loop
PERF-015.description: Performs repeated numeric-to-string conversion inside loops where buffered writes, cached conversion, or batching would be cheaper.
PERF-015.original_description: Detect strconv.Itoa, FormatInt, FormatUint, or FormatFloat inside loop bodies.
PERF-015.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match repeated strconv formatting in loops and suppress when the cost is clearly amortized or the loop is not on a hot path.

PERF-016.name: Buffer Reallocation Inside Loop
PERF-016.description: Allocates a fresh bytes.Buffer inside an inner loop instead of reusing a resettable buffer across iterations.
PERF-016.original_description: Detect bytes.Buffer{} or new(bytes.Buffer) inside loop bodies when reuse is viable.
PERF-016.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match buffer allocation in loops and suppress when the allocation is one-shot by design or when reset-based reuse is not valid for the code shape.

PERF-017.name: String Concatenation With Plus Inside Loop
PERF-017.description: Builds a string with the + operator inside a loop, causing repeated allocation and copy churn. Use strings.Builder or a pre-sized buffer.
PERF-017.original_description: Detect s = s + x or s += x inside a for or range loop body.
PERF-017.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match string += inside loop bodies and suppress when the loop is bounded and tiny, or when strings.Builder is already used.

PERF-018.name: Unnecessary Slice Copy On Reslice
PERF-018.description: Copies a slice header into a function that only needs a sub-range, causing the full backing array to remain live and increase GC pressure.
PERF-018.original_description: Detect passing a large slice where a sub-slice expression would suffice, or full copy where append-to-self would work.
PERF-018.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match large slice passing patterns and suppress when the callee mutates the slice in place or escapes it.

PERF-019.name: Large Value Copy In Range Loop
PERF-019.description: Iterates over a slice of large structs by value, copying each element. Range by index or pointer to avoid per-iteration copies.
PERF-019.original_description: Detect for _, v := range largeStructSlice where the element type is a non-trivial struct.
PERF-019.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match range by value over large structs and suppress when the copy is intentional for value semantics.

PERF-020.name: Reflection Usage In Hot Path
PERF-020.description: Uses the reflect package on a hot request path. Reflection is significantly slower than typed or codegen-based dispatch.
PERF-020.original_description: Detect reflect.ValueOf, reflect.TypeOf, reflect.New, or method lookup via reflect on request-serving paths.
PERF-020.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match reflect usage on hot paths and suppress when code is initialization-only, codegen, or generic helper that runs once.

PERF-021.name: io.ReadAll On Large Request Body
PERF-021.description: Uses io.ReadAll to fully buffer a request body that may be large. This materializes the whole body in memory and can amplify memory pressure.
PERF-021.original_description: Detect io.ReadAll, ioutil.ReadAll, or io.ReadFull on http.Request.Body in handlers.
PERF-021.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match io.ReadAll on request bodies and suppress when bodies are small or already bounded by middleware.

PERF-022.name: os.ReadFile Inside Handler
PERF-022.description: Reads an entire file inside a request handler on every call. Large or remote files cause repeated disk I/O on a hot path.
PERF-022.original_description: Detect os.ReadFile, ioutil.ReadFile, or os.ReadFile in handler or middleware functions.
PERF-022.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match file reads in handler paths and suppress when file is small config or already cached via sync.Once.

PERF-023.name: bytes.NewReader Allocation Per Request
PERF-023.description: Allocates a new bytes.Reader or bytes.Buffer for each request when a sync.Pool or reusable buffer would be cheaper.
PERF-023.original_description: Detect bytes.NewReader, bytes.NewBuffer, or new(bytes.Buffer) on request-serving paths.
PERF-023.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match per-request buffer allocation and suppress when the buffer is intentionally short-lived or escapes into goroutines.

PERF-024.name: Crypto Hashing In Tight Loop
PERF-024.description: Creates a new hash instance (sha256, sha1, md5) inside a tight loop where the hasher could be reset or hoisted.
PERF-024.original_description: Detect sha256.New, sha1.New, md5.New, or hmac.New inside loop bodies or per-row hot paths.
PERF-024.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match per-iteration hasher creation and suppress when the loop is bounded or already uses a pooled hasher.

PERF-025.name: RSA Key Generation Per Request
PERF-025.description: Generates RSA keys inside request handlers or per-call paths. RSA key generation is expensive and should be cached.
PERF-025.original_description: Detect rsa.GenerateKey, rsa.GenerateMultiPrimeKey, or ecdsa.GenerateKey in handlers or middleware.
PERF-025.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match key generation in hot paths and suppress when keys are intentionally rotated or generated only at startup.

PERF-026.name: Base64 Encode Or Decode In Loop
PERF-026.description: Performs base64 encoding or decoding repeatedly inside a loop. Encoders and decoders can be reused, and streaming is often cheaper.
PERF-026.original_description: Detect base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString, DecodeString, NewEncoder, or NewDecoder inside loop bodies.
PERF-026.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match base64 conversion in loops and suppress when input sizes are tiny or the conversion is one-shot.

PERF-027.name: Missed sync.Pool Reuse Opportunity
PERF-027.description: Allocates short-lived buffers or objects on hot paths without using sync.Pool, increasing GC pressure.
PERF-027.original_description: Detect allocation of bytes.Buffer, []byte, or frequently used structs in hot paths without a sync.Pool wrapper.
PERF-027.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match per-call buffer allocation patterns and suppress when objects escape to other goroutines or are stored beyond the request.

PERF-028.name: Sync Mutex Allocation Per Request
PERF-028.description: Embeds or constructs a sync.Mutex per request or per struct instance where a shared mutex or atomic primitive would be cheaper.
PERF-028.original_description: Detect sync.Mutex{}, sync.RWMutex{}, or pointer-embedded mutexes inside request-scoped or per-record structs.
PERF-028.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match mutex-per-instance patterns and suppress when per-record locking is the documented concurrency model.

PERF-029.name: Unbounded Goroutine Spawn
PERF-029.description: Spawns goroutines without a worker pool, semaphore, or bounded concurrency, which can exhaust memory and file descriptors under load.
PERF-029.original_description: Detect go func(){} in a loop, in a request handler, or inside a fan-out pattern with no semaphore or worker pool.
PERF-029.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match unconstrained go statements and suppress when the input set is bounded or the goroutine is part of a worker pool.

PERF-030.name: context.Background In Goroutine From Request
PERF-030.description: Creates a new context.Background() inside a goroutine launched from a request handler, breaking cancellation and request-scoped values.
PERF-030.original_description: Detect context.Background() or context.TODO() in goroutines spawned from handlers or middleware.
PERF-030.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match context.Background() in goroutine launches and suppress when the context is intentionally detached from the parent request.

PERF-031.name: Defer In Hot Function
PERF-031.description: Uses defer in performance-sensitive hot functions where the per-call overhead is measurable. defer has a small but non-zero cost in Go 1.13 and earlier.
PERF-031.original_description: Detect defer calls in functions that are called in tight loops or on request paths where profiling shows hot frames.
PERF-031.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match defer usage in hot paths and suppress when readability outweighs the micro-overhead.

PERF-032.name: String Byte Conversion In Hot Path
PERF-032.description: Converts between string and []byte repeatedly using []byte(s) or string(b), which copies the underlying data.
PERF-032.original_description: Detect []byte(str) or string(byteSlice) inside loop bodies or per-request hot paths.
PERF-032.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match string/byte conversions in hot paths and suppress when copy is required by API or when unsafe zero-copy is not safe.

PERF-033.name: Range Over Large Slice With Early Break Missing
PERF-033.description: Iterates over a large slice without using indexed access, missing opportunities for early termination or two-pointer patterns.
PERF-033.original_description: Detect range over large slices in handlers, batch processors, or search code where indexed scan would be more efficient.
PERF-033.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match range-over-slice patterns and suppress when iteration is clean enough to justify the readability.

PERF-034.name: Map Iteration With Append
PERF-034.description: Appends to a slice inside a map iteration, which is safe but often indicates accidental quadratic copying when the slice grows large.
PERF-034.original_description: Detect append inside a for k,v := range map body where the slice grows without preallocation.
PERF-034.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match append inside map ranges and suppress when the map is small or preallocation is already correct.

PERF-035.name: Interface Boxing On Hot Path
PERF-035.description: Passes concrete values into interface{} parameters on hot paths, causing escape-to-heap and dynamic dispatch overhead.
PERF-035.original_description: Detect fmt.Sprintf with non-string args, or interface{}-typed function parameters on hot paths.
PERF-035.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match interface{} boxing and suppress when the API requires it or when the cost is amortized.

PERF-036.name: Loop Variable Capture In Goroutine
PERF-036.description: Captures a loop variable by reference inside a goroutine (pre-Go 1.22 semantics), causing all goroutines to see the final value.
PERF-036.original_description: Detect go func(){ use(v) }() where v is a for/range loop variable without a per-iteration copy or shadowing.
PERF-036.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match goroutines that capture loop variables and suppress when the variable is correctly copied or the project targets Go 1.22+.

PERF-037.name: Slice Growth Miscalculation
PERF-037.description: Repeatedly appends to a slice without preallocation or with insufficient capacity hint, causing repeated reallocation and copy.
PERF-037.original_description: Detect append with no make + cap hint in a loop or function that grows a large slice.
PERF-037.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match append in hot grow patterns and suppress when the final size is unknown or already preallocated.

PERF-038.name: Unbuffered Channel In Producer Consumer
PERF-038.description: Uses an unbuffered channel for high-throughput producer/consumer patterns, causing unnecessary blocking and synchronization.
PERF-038.original_description: Detect make(chan T) in producer/consumer pipelines where buffered channels would smooth throughput.
PERF-038.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match unbuffered channels in hot pipelines and suppress when strict synchronization is required by design.

PERF-039.name: Select With Default In Tight Loop
PERF-039.description: Uses select with a default case in a tight loop, which becomes a busy-wait pattern that pins a CPU core.
PERF-039.original_description: Detect for { select { case ...; default: ... } } in polling or worker loops.
PERF-039.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match select-default in loops and suppress when a small intentional sleep or backoff is also present.

PERF-040.name: time.Now Calls In Hot Path
PERF-040.description: Calls time.Now() repeatedly inside hot paths where a single timestamp at function entry would suffice.
PERF-040.original_description: Detect time.Now() called multiple times in the same function body, especially inside loops.
PERF-040.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match multiple time.Now calls and suppress when distinct timestamps are required by the logic.

PERF-041.name: log Standard Logger In Production Hot Path
PERF-041.description: Uses the standard log package or log.Println/Printf on production hot paths, where structured, leveled, or async loggers are cheaper.
PERF-041.original_description: Detect log.Println, log.Printf, log.Print inside handler or loop bodies.
PERF-041.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match standard log usage in hot paths and suppress when the project intentionally uses the standard log package or the call is gated by verbosity.

PERF-042.name: fmt.Errorf With No Formatting In Hot Path
PERF-042.description: Uses fmt.Errorf for error wrapping without format verbs, which is slower than errors.New and requires extra parsing.
PERF-042.original_description: Detect fmt.Errorf("static message") where the string contains no %s, %d, %v, or other format verbs.
PERF-042.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match fmt.Errorf with no verbs and suppress when formatting is added for consistency.

PERF-043.name: Panic Recovery In Hot Path
PERF-043.description: Recovers from panics in tight loops or hot functions, which adds defer setup and runtime cost on every iteration.
PERF-043.original_description: Detect defer func(){ recover() }() inside hot loops or per-request paths.
PERF-043.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match defer-recover in hot paths and suppress when the boundary is meaningful or the recover is required for safety.

PERF-044.name: Repeated Type Assertion On Same Interface
PERF-044.description: Performs the same type assertion on an interface value multiple times, where a single assertion with reuse would be cheaper.
PERF-044.original_description: Detect x.(T) called multiple times for the same value inside a function or loop body.
PERF-044.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match repeated type assertions and suppress when assertion success is the common path that the type system cannot optimize.

PERF-045.name: Append Without Capacity Hint In Loop
PERF-045.description: Appends to a slice inside a loop without setting an initial capacity, leading to repeated reallocation as the slice grows geometrically.
PERF-045.original_description: Detect append in a for/range loop where the slice has no make([]T, 0, hint) before the loop.
PERF-045.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match append-in-loop without preallocation and suppress when the final size is genuinely unknown.

PERF-046.name: String Trimming With Allocations
PERF-046.description: Uses strings.TrimSpace or similar trimming functions on hot paths where the result is the same as the input, allocating a new string.
PERF-046.original_description: Detect strings.TrimSpace, Trim, TrimPrefix, TrimSuffix in hot paths without checking the need first.
PERF-046.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match string trimming in hot paths and suppress when correctness requires trimming.

PERF-047.name: strings.Split In Hot Path
PERF-047.description: Uses strings.Split or strings.SplitN in hot paths where a streaming scanner or precomputed index would be cheaper.
PERF-047.original_description: Detect strings.Split, strings.SplitN, or strings.SplitAfter inside hot loops or per-request parsing.
PERF-047.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match strings.Split in hot paths and suppress when input is small or splitting semantics are required.

PERF-048.name: bytes.Equal On Long Strings
PERF-048.description: Compares long byte slices or strings without short-circuit optimizations, where length or hash precheck would help.
PERF-048.original_description: Detect bytes.Equal or strings.EqualFold on long inputs in hot paths without an early length or prefix check.
PERF-048.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match byte/string equality in hot paths and suppress when the full compare is required for correctness.

PERF-049.name: Copy With Mismatched Length
PERF-049.description: Uses copy(dst, src) where the destination length is not checked, leading to silent truncation or wasted capacity.
PERF-049.original_description: Detect copy(dst, src) where dst was allocated with a wrong cap or where bounds are not validated.
PERF-049.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match copy calls in hot paths and suppress when lengths are explicitly validated upstream.

PERF-050.name: regexp.MatchString In Loop
PERF-050.description: Calls regexp.MatchString (or similar regex match helpers) inside a loop instead of compiling once and reusing the pattern.
PERF-050.original_description: Detect regexp.MatchString or regexp.Match inside loop bodies or per-row processing.
PERF-050.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match regexp.MatchString in loops and suppress when the pattern is genuinely dynamic per iteration.

PERF-051.name: unsafe.Pointer Misuse Pattern
PERF-051.description: Uses unsafe.Pointer for type punning or zero-copy conversion in ways that hurt readability and may cause subtle performance issues when miscompiled by future Go versions.
PERF-051.original_description: Detect unsafe.Pointer usage in hot paths without clear justification, especially for string/byte conversion.
PERF-051.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match unsafe.Pointer usage and suppress when a benchmark justifies the pattern or when no safe alternative exists.

PERF-052.name: Manual runtime.GC Call
PERF-052.description: Calls runtime.GC() in production code, forcing a stop-the-world GC pause and undermining the runtime's tuning.
PERF-052.original_description: Detect runtime.GC() calls outside of tests, debug builds, or initialization shutdown paths.
PERF-052.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match runtime.GC() calls and suppress when used in tests, controlled shutdown, or where deterministic GC is required.

PERF-053.name: math/rand Without Explicit Source
PERF-053.description: Uses the package-level math/rand functions, which are guarded by a global mutex and can become a contention hotspot.
PERF-053.original_description: Detect math/rand.Intn, math/rand.Float64, or rand.Read on hot paths where a per-goroutine rand.Source would be cheaper.
PERF-053.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match package-level math/rand in hot paths and suppress when usage is sparse or mutex contention is not a concern.

PERF-054.name: strings.Builder Reset Missed
PERF-054.description: Allocates a new strings.Builder per request instead of calling Reset on a pooled or reused builder.
PERF-054.original_description: Detect strings.Builder{} inside hot paths where a sync.Pool or hoisted builder with Reset would be cheaper.
PERF-054.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match new strings.Builder allocations on hot paths and suppress when the builder intentionally escapes or is one-shot.

PERF-055.name: bufio.Scanner Default Token Size
PERF-055.description: Uses bufio.Scanner with the default 64KiB max token size on potentially large input, which silently truncates or errors out.
PERF-055.original_description: Detect bufio.NewScanner with no explicit Buffer call before Scan, on inputs that may exceed 64KiB.
PERF-055.detection_notes: Future Go performance heuristic. Match bufio.Scanner without explicit buffer sizing and suppress when input is bounded or already configured upstream.

PERF-056.name: Gin c.JSON With Marshal In Loop
PERF-056.description: Calls c.JSON inside a loop with on-the-fly marshaling per iteration. Marshaling per iteration defeats batch response strategies and can balloon CPU under load.
PERF-056.original_description: Detect c.JSON or c.JSONP inside for/range loops in Gin handlers.
PERF-056.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match c.JSON in loop bodies and suppress when the loop is a streaming response or has been intentionally serialized.

PERF-057.name: Gin Middleware Heavy Allocation
PERF-057.description: Defines middleware that allocates large buffers, parses bodies, or builds heavy structures for every request even when downstream handlers do not need them.
PERF-057.original_description: Detect gin.HandlerFunc implementations with io.ReadAll, large make, or json.Unmarshal at the middleware level.
PERF-057.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match heavy work in middleware and suppress when the work is gated by routing or required for downstream safety.

PERF-058.name: Gin c.Request.Body Not Closed
PERF-058.description: Reads c.Request.Body without deferring Close, causing connection leaks and request body buffer retention.
PERF-058.original_description: Detect c.Request.Body access without a matching defer body.Close() or io.Copy to discard.
PERF-058.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match body reads without close and suppress when middleware handles closing globally.

PERF-059.name: Gin Binding Reuse Missed
PERF-059.description: Uses c.ShouldBindJSON, c.ShouldBind, or c.Bind on every request even when the same DTO is reused, missing typed handler or struct reuse opportunities.
PERF-059.original_description: Detect c.ShouldBindJSON or c.ShouldBind in handlers that could share a pre-validated struct or per-route binder.
PERF-059.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match binding calls and suppress when input shapes vary or when binding is required for validation.

PERF-060.name: Gin Render Allocation Per Request
PERF-060.description: Allocates a new render.Render or per-request render struct instead of relying on the default render pool Gin's c.Render manages.
PERF-060.original_description: Detect render.JSON or render.HTML direct allocation inside Gin handlers.
PERF-060.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match direct render usage and suppress when a custom render is intentional or required for streaming.

PERF-061.name: Gin Static Handler No Cache Headers
PERF-061.description: Serves static files via gin.Static or router.StaticFS without Cache-Control, ETag, or Last-Modified headers, forcing redundant client fetches.
PERF-061.original_description: Detect gin.Static, router.Static, or c.File usage without explicit cache header configuration.
PERF-061.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match static serving and suppress when caching is handled by a CDN or reverse proxy in front of the service.

PERF-062.name: Gin Route Parameter Parsing In Middleware
PERF-062.description: Parses complex route parameters (e.g., UUIDs, JSON) inside middleware that runs for every request, even for routes that do not need them.
PERF-062.original_description: Detect c.Param parsing with regex or json.Unmarshal inside gin.HandlerFunc middleware.
PERF-062.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match param parsing in middleware and suppress when it is required by the routing model.

PERF-063.name: Gin Custom Validator Allocation
PERF-063.description: Registers or invokes custom validator factories on every request instead of initializing them once via binding.Validator.Engine().
PERF-063.original_description: Detect binding.Validator.Engine() usage inside handlers or repeated validator construction.
PERF-063.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match validator allocation patterns and suppress when validators are intentionally reconfigured per request.

PERF-064.name: Gin c.Copy Not Called In Goroutine
PERF-064.description: Spawns a goroutine that uses *gin.Context directly. The context's underlying objects are pooled and will be recycled, causing data races and corruption.
PERF-064.original_description: Detect go func() { c.JSON(...) }() or similar goroutine usage of *gin.Context without c.Copy().
PERF-064.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match goroutine use of *gin.Context and suppress when the project is on Gin v1.10+ with context.Copy semantics or uses c.Request.Context() only.

PERF-065.name: Gin ShouldBind In Middleware Chain
PERF-065.description: Calls c.ShouldBind in a middleware that runs before route dispatch, performing the same parse work for routes that may not need it.
PERF-065.original_description: Detect c.ShouldBind or c.ShouldBindJSON inside RouterGroup.Use handlers or shared middleware.
PERF-065.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match binding in middleware and suppress when binding is required for the entire downstream chain.

PERF-066.name: Gin Deep Middleware Chain
PERF-066.description: Builds a deep middleware chain per route group where many middlewares can be merged or composed, adding per-request frame overhead.
PERF-066.original_description: Detect router.Group(...).Use(...) called with more than ~5 middlewares or chained .Use on nested groups.
PERF-066.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match deep middleware chains and suppress when separation of concerns is required for security or audit.

PERF-067.name: Gin Recovery Middleware Disabled
PERF-067.description: Removes gin.Recovery() in production, allowing panics to crash workers. While strictly a reliability issue, repeated panics under load have severe performance impact.
PERF-067.original_description: Detect gin.New() without gin.Recovery() or r.Use(gin.Recovery()) on the production router.
PERF-067.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match routers without recovery middleware and suppress when an external supervisor provides recovery semantics.

PERF-068.name: Gin Logger Middleware In Production Hot Path
PERF-068.description: Leaves gin.Logger() or gin.LoggerWithFormatter in production, writing a log line per request with synchronous I/O on the hot path.
PERF-068.original_description: Detect gin.Logger() in router setup or gin.LoggerWithConfig with stdout sink.
PERF-068.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match synchronous logger middleware and suppress when access logs are required and rate-limited by design.

PERF-069.name: Gin c.Writer Buffer Flush Missed
PERF-069.description: Streams a large response with c.Writer without periodic flush, increasing time-to-first-byte for clients.
PERF-069.original_description: Detect c.Writer.Write or c.Stream usage without c.Writer.Flush() or SSE-style flushing.
PERF-069.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match streaming responses without flush and suppress when the response is short and the buffer is automatically flushed on handler exit.

PERF-070.name: Gin Handler Goroutine Leak
PERF-070.description: Spawns goroutines inside a Gin handler without ensuring they finish before the response is returned, leading to goroutine leaks and resource retention.
PERF-070.original_description: Detect go func(){} in gin.HandlerFunc without a sync.WaitGroup, done channel, or context cancellation tied to the request.
PERF-070.detection_notes: Future Gin performance heuristic. Match goroutine spawn in handlers and suppress when the goroutine is correctly tied to request lifetime via context or wait group.

PERF-071.name: GORM N+1 Query Pattern
PERF-071.description: Loads a list of parent records and then queries related records one by one inside a loop, producing N+1 round-trips to the database.
PERF-071.original_description: Detect db.Find or .First inside a for loop iterating over a previously loaded list, without Preload.
PERF-071.detection_notes: Future GORM performance heuristic. Match query-in-loop patterns and suppress when batching is explicitly not possible or already done via Joins or Preload.

PERF-072.name: GORM Transaction Per Request
PERF-072.description: Wraps every request in a database transaction, adding BEGIN/COMMIT round-trip overhead even for read-only or single-statement paths.
PERF-072.original_description: Detect db.Transaction(...) or tx := db.Begin() inside handlers where the work is read-only or single-statement.
PERF-072.detection_notes: Future GORM performance heuristic. Match per-request transactions and suppress when correctness requires atomicity.

PERF-073.name: GORM Preload Missing For Relations
PERF-073.description: Queries a parent record and accesses a relation in code paths, triggering lazy loads on the hot path. Preload or Joins would be cheaper.
PERF-073.original_description: Detect db.First, db.Find, or db.Take followed by access to a relation field without Preload or Joins.
PERF-073.detection_notes: Future GORM performance heuristic. Match relation access without preload and suppress when relations are intentionally lazy.

PERF-074.name: GORM Select All Columns
PERF-074.description: Uses db.Find or unscoped queries without Select(), pulling all columns including large text or blob fields that the response does not need.
PERF-074.original_description: Detect db.Find, db.First, db.Take, or db.Where without a Select clause in handlers that only project a subset of fields.
PERF-074.detection_notes: Future GORM performance heuristic. Match unbounded selects and suppress when the full record is required.

PERF-075.name: GORM Session Not Reused
PERF-075.description: Configures GORM session options (logger, dry run, prepared statement) per call instead of once via Session or a shared session config.
PERF-075.original_description: Detect db.Session(&gorm.Session{...}) inside handlers with per-request options that could be hoisted.
PERF-075.detection_notes: Future GORM performance heuristic. Match per-request session config and suppress when per-request overrides are intentionally required.

PERF-076.name: GORM Create In Loop
PERF-076.description: Inserts records one at a time with db.Create inside a loop. Bulk insert with db.CreateInBatches or a single multi-value INSERT is significantly faster.
PERF-076.original_description: Detect db.Create(...) inside a for/range loop without batching.
PERF-076.detection_notes: Future GORM performance heuristic. Match per-iteration Create and suppress when per-row hooks or generated IDs are required.

PERF-077.name: GORM Save Vs Update Misuse
PERF-077.description: Uses db.Save for partial updates, which performs an UPSERT (UPDATE all columns or INSERT). db.Update or db.Updates with explicit fields is more efficient for partial updates.
PERF-077.original_description: Detect db.Save in update-only code paths where only a subset of fields changes.
PERF-077.detection_notes: Future GORM performance heuristic. Match db.Save for partial updates and suppress when full-row replacement is intentional.

PERF-078.name: GORM Raw Query Missing Index Hint
PERF-078.description: Builds raw SQL with db.Raw or db.Exec on columns that lack indexes, leading to sequential scans on large tables.
PERF-078.original_description: Detect db.Raw or db.Exec with WHERE, ORDER BY, or JOIN clauses that are not backed by indexes visible in migration files.
PERF-078.detection_notes: Future GORM performance heuristic. Match raw queries on unindexed columns and suppress when index coverage is verified externally.

PERF-079.name: GORM Connection Pool Exhaustion
PERF-079.description: Misses db.SetMaxOpenConns, db.SetMaxIdleConns, or db.SetConnMaxLifetime configuration, leading to pool exhaustion or stale connections.
PERF-079.original_description: Detect *sql.DB or *gorm.DB that is never configured with SetMaxOpenConns, SetMaxIdleConns, or SetConnMaxLifetime.
PERF-079.detection_notes: Future GORM performance heuristic. Match unconfigured pools and suppress when defaults are intentionally relied on or set elsewhere.

PERF-080.name: GORM Pluck Returns Large Slice
PERF-080.description: Uses db.Pluck to load a single column from a large table into a slice in memory, which can balloon memory usage.
PERF-080.original_description: Detect db.Pluck or distinct queries on large tables without limit or batching.
PERF-080.detection_notes: Future GORM performance heuristic. Match Pluck without limit and suppress when result set is bounded by a where clause or batched.

PERF-081.name: sqlx SelectIn Unbounded Slice
PERF-081.description: Builds an IN clause with a large slice in sqlx, generating a query so large it hits DB parameter limits or performs poorly.
PERF-081.original_description: Detect db.Select or db.Queryx with a slice expanded into an IN clause without chunking.
PERF-081.detection_notes: Future sqlx performance heuristic. Match unbounded IN slices and suppress when chunking is already applied or input is bounded.

PERF-082.name: sqlx StructScan In Loop
PERF-082.description: Calls rows.StructScan inside a loop without amortizing reflection. Caching struct scan targets or using Get/SELECT INTO is cheaper.
PERF-082.original_description: Detect rows.StructScan inside for rows.Next() loops where the destination type is large or repeated.
PERF-082.detection_notes: Future sqlx performance heuristic. Match StructScan in row loops and suppress when the destination type varies per row.

PERF-083.name: sqlx MapScan Allocation
PERF-083.description: Uses MapScan in hot paths where typed scanning or reuse would be cheaper, since MapScan always allocates a new map per row.
PERF-083.original_description: Detect rows.MapScan inside row iteration loops on hot paths.
PERF-083.detection_notes: Future sqlx performance heuristic. Match MapScan in hot paths and suppress when dynamic schemas require the map form.

PERF-084.name: sqlx Transaction Misuse
PERF-084.description: Mixes Begin/Commit patterns with sqlx incorrectly, holding a transaction longer than needed or nesting transactions without savepoints.
PERF-084.original_description: Detect db.Beginx, db.MustBegin, or tx inside handlers where the work could be done in a single statement or shorter transaction.
PERF-084.detection_notes: Future sqlx performance heuristic. Match transaction boundaries and suppress when atomicity requires the current scope.

PERF-085.name: sqlx Named Query Binding Cost
PERF-085.description: Uses sqlx.Named or sqlx.In repeatedly with large parameter sets, which builds query strings and argument lists per call.
PERF-085.original_description: Detect sqlx.Named, sqlx.In, or named Exec in tight loops where the query shape is stable.
PERF-085.detection_notes: Future sqlx performance heuristic. Match named query construction and suppress when arguments vary legitimately per call.

PERF-086.name: Echo c.JSON Marshal In Hot Path
PERF-086.description: Calls c.JSON inside Echo handlers with on-the-fly marshaling per request, missing pooled encoder opportunities.
PERF-086.original_description: Detect c.JSON or c.JSONP inside Echo handlers that could batch or pool encoding state.
PERF-086.detection_notes: Future Echo performance heuristic. Match c.JSON in hot handlers and suppress when the response shape is dynamic or already optimized.

PERF-087.name: Echo Bind Without Validation Reuse
PERF-087.description: Calls c.Bind on every request with full validation enabled, even for trusted internal routes that only need parsing.
PERF-087.original_description: Detect c.Bind or c.BindWith inside Echo handlers with DefaultBinder and full validation in trusted paths.
PERF-087.detection_notes: Future Echo performance heuristic. Match binding with validation on hot paths and suppress when validation is required for security.

PERF-088.name: Echo Static Handler Missing Cache
PERF-088.description: Serves static files via echo.Static without Cache-Control, ETag, or Last-Modified headers, forcing redundant client fetches.
PERF-088.original_description: Detect echo.Static, e.File, or static middleware without cache header configuration.
PERF-088.detection_notes: Future Echo performance heuristic. Match static serving and suppress when caching is handled by a CDN or reverse proxy.

PERF-089.name: Echo Middleware Allocation
PERF-089.description: Defines middleware that allocates large structures on every request, even when the downstream handler does not need them.
PERF-089.original_description: Detect echo.MiddlewareFunc implementations with large make, json.Unmarshal, or per-request struct allocation at the middleware level.
PERF-089.detection_notes: Future Echo performance heuristic. Match heavy middleware work and suppress when the work is gated by routing or required for downstream safety.

PERF-090.name: Echo Context Store Growth
PERF-090.description: Stores unbounded keys in c.Set() across the request lifetime, retaining memory until the context is recycled.
PERF-090.original_description: Detect c.Set("key", largeValue) inside Echo middleware with values that are large or accumulate.
PERF-090.detection_notes: Future Echo performance heuristic. Match c.Set usage and suppress when the value is small or required for downstream handler.

PERF-091.name: Fiber fasthttp Pooling Miss
PERF-091.description: Allocates per-request buffers or objects in Fiber handlers without using fasthttp's pooling primitives like *fasthttp.RequestCtx byte slices or sync.Pool.
PERF-091.original_description: Detect c.Request.Body(), c.Response.BodyWriter(), or byte buffer allocation inside Fiber handlers without pool reuse.
PERF-091.detection_notes: Future Fiber performance heuristic. Match per-request buffer allocation in Fiber handlers and suppress when the allocation is one-shot or escapes correctly.

PERF-092.name: Fiber Ctx Allocation Misuse
PERF-092.description: Stores request-scoped data in a *fiber.Ctx field or returns the ctx from a goroutine, missing the fact that fiber.Ctx is reused per request.
PERF-092.original_description: Detect go func(){ use(c) }() or storing c beyond the request lifetime in Fiber handlers.
PERF-092.detection_notes: Future Fiber performance heuristic. Match c usage in goroutines and suppress when c.UserContext is used correctly.

PERF-093.name: Fiber JSON Encoder Reuse Missed
PERF-093.description: Allocates a new json.Encoder or json.Marshal call per response when a pooled encoder would be cheaper.
PERF-093.original_description: Detect c.JSON or json.NewEncoder allocation inside Fiber handlers in hot paths.
PERF-093.detection_notes: Future Fiber performance heuristic. Match JSON allocation per request and suppress when the response is dynamic or already optimized.

PERF-094.name: Fiber Request Body Read Pattern
PERF-094.description: Reads the Fiber request body in a way that triggers buffer copies, missing fasthttp's zero-copy PostArgs or BodyStream access.
PERF-094.original_description: Detect c.Body() or io.ReadAll on c.RequestBodyStream() in Fiber handlers where fasthttp byte slices would suffice.
PERF-094.detection_notes: Future Fiber performance heuristic. Match body reads in handlers and suppress when copy is required for downstream safety.

PERF-095.name: Fiber Middleware Chain Growth
PERF-095.description: Builds a deep middleware chain per route group in Fiber, where fasthttp per-request overhead multiplies with chain depth.
PERF-095.original_description: Detect app.Use(...) called repeatedly or nested route groups with overlapping middleware in Fiber apps.
PERF-095.detection_notes: Future Fiber performance heuristic. Match deep middleware chains and suppress when separation of concerns is required.

PERF-096.name: gRPC Stream Allocation Per Recv
PERF-096.description: Allocates a new message struct per Recv in a gRPC client stream, where reusing a single message would reduce GC pressure.
PERF-096.original_description: Detect for { msg := NewProtoType(); stream.RecvMsg(msg) } or similar per-call allocation in gRPC clients.
PERF-096.detection_notes: Future gRPC performance heuristic. Match per-recv allocation and suppress when the message escapes the loop or has lifetime constraints.

PERF-097.name: protobuf Marshal In Loop
PERF-097.description: Marshals protobuf messages inside a loop without reusing marshaling buffers or using proto.MarshalOptions with deterministic state.
PERF-097.original_description: Detect proto.Marshal or jsonpb.Marshaler in tight loops without buffer reuse.
PERF-097.detection_notes: Future protobuf performance heuristic. Match marshal in loops and suppress when message shape varies legitimately.

PERF-098.name: go-redis Pipeline Missed
PERF-098.description: Issues sequential redis commands in a loop, missing the chance to pipeline them into a single round-trip.
PERF-098.original_description: Detect rdb.Set, rdb.Get, or similar redis calls inside a for/range loop without Pipeline().
PERF-098.detection_notes: Future go-redis performance heuristic. Match sequential redis calls and suppress when ordering or atomicity requires single calls.

PERF-099.name: Prometheus Label High Cardinality
PERF-099.description: Defines a counter, gauge, or histogram with a high-cardinality label (user ID, request ID, full URL) that explodes time series storage.
PERF-099.original_description: Detect prometheus.NewCounterVec or NewHistogramVec with labels that include user IDs, UUIDs, or unbounded path parameters.
PERF-099.detection_notes: Future Prometheus performance heuristic. Match high-cardinality labels and suppress when label space is bounded by external contract.

PERF-100.name: Cobra Command Subcommand Allocation
PERF-100.description: Registers cobra subcommands with expensive Run hooks, large init logic, or per-invocation flag parsing that could be amortized.
PERF-100.original_description: Detect cobra.Command with heavy RunE, large init blocks, or repeated flag registration patterns in CLI tools.
PERF-100.detection_notes: Future cobra performance heuristic. Match heavy init in Run hooks and suppress when CLI startup cost is acceptable for the use case.

PERF-101.name: HTTP Server Timeouts Not Configured
PERF-101.description: An http.Server is created without ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, or IdleTimeout configured. Without these timeouts, slow clients can hold connections indefinitely, exhausting goroutines and file descriptors under load. This is a Slowloris-style DoS vector in production Go services.
PERF-101.detection_notes: Match http.Server composite literals that do not set ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, or IdleTimeout fields. Suppress when a reverse proxy handles timeout enforcement.

PERF-102.name: HTTP Transport Not Shared Across Requests
PERF-102.description: Creates a new http.Transport instance per http.Client instead of sharing a single transport across clients. The http.Transport maintains a connection pool for keep-alive and TLS session resumption; creating new transports fragments this pool and causes redundant TLS handshakes.
PERF-102.original_description: Detect http.Transport created per-client instead of shared globally across HTTP clients
PERF-102.detection_notes: Match http.Transport{} composite literals; check if they are assigned to a globally shared variable or recreated per-client.

PERF-103.name: HTTP Response Body Not Closed
PERF-103.description: Failing to close http.Response.Body leaks TCP connections from the connection pool. Even if all bytes are read, the connection cannot be reused for keep-alive unless the body is fully consumed AND closed. Over time this causes connection pool exhaustion and file descriptor leaks.
PERF-103.original_description: Detect http.Response.Body not closed after client.Do/Get/Post
PERF-103.detection_notes: Find calls to client.Do, client.Get, http.Get, http.Post where the response Body is not followed by defer resp.Body.Close() or explicit Body.Close() in the same function scope.

PERF-104.name: WriteHeader Called Multiple Times In Handler
PERF-104.description: Calls w.WriteHeader() multiple times in a single HTTP handler. The first call sets the status code; subsequent calls are silently ignored but still execute, wasting CPU on a no-op. This often indicates confused error-handling logic where multiple code paths attempt to write the response status.
PERF-104.original_description: Detect multiple WriteHeader calls on the same ResponseWriter in one handler
PERF-104.detection_notes: Match two or more w.WriteHeader calls on the same http.ResponseWriter in a single handler function.

PERF-105.name: runtime.SetFinalizer On Hot Path Object
PERF-105.description: Uses runtime.SetFinalizer on objects created frequently in hot paths. Objects with finalizers require an extra GC cycle to collect (the object is resurrected for the finalizer, then collected on the next cycle). This doubles GC work for those objects and adds them to a global finalizer list.
PERF-105.original_description: Detect runtime.SetFinalizer usage on objects created in request handlers or hot loops
PERF-105.detection_notes: Match runtime.SetFinalizer calls reachable from request handlers or hot loops. Prefer explicit Close methods with finalizers only as safety nets.

PERF-106.name: sync.Map Used For Write-Heavy Workload
PERF-106.description: Uses sync.Map for workloads dominated by writes (Store) and updates. sync.Map is optimized for read-heavy, key-stable workloads with its internal read/dirty dual-map structure. Under frequent writes, the dirty map promotion cost outweighs the concurrency benefits compared to a plain map with sync.Mutex.
PERF-106.original_description: Detect sync.Map in write-heavy patterns where plain map + mutex is faster
PERF-106.detection_notes: Match sync.Map declarations where Store/LoadAndDelete calls outnumber Load calls in the same scope.

PERF-107.name: encoding/binary Write Or Read Inside Loop
PERF-107.description: Uses encoding/binary.Write or binary.Read inside a loop. These functions use reflection to determine the size and layout of the target type on every call. For fixed-size types, manual BigEndian.PutUint32 or LittleEndian.Uint32 methods avoid reflection and are 10-20x faster.
PERF-107.original_description: Detect binary.Read or binary.Write inside loop bodies where manual endian methods would avoid reflection
PERF-107.detection_notes: Match binary.Read or binary.Write call expressions inside for/range loop bodies where the data parameter is a fixed-size basic type.

PERF-108.name: sort.Search Repeated In Loop
PERF-108.description: Calls sort.Search inside a loop with a function literal closure, allocating a new closure on every iteration. For repeated binary searches on the same sorted data, extract the predicate as a named function or pre-build a lookup structure to avoid per-call closure allocation.
PERF-108.original_description: Detect sort.Search with closure inside loop bodies
PERF-108.detection_notes: Match sort.Search calls inside for/range loop bodies where the second argument is a function literal.

PERF-109.name: Map Key Recomputed In Loop Without Caching
PERF-109.description: Recomputes a map key inside a loop by calling an expensive function (string formatting, hashing, encoding) on every iteration when the key could be computed once and stored. Each recomputation adds CPU overhead proportional to the key computation cost times the loop count.
PERF-109.original_description: Detect repeated expensive map key computation inside loops where caching would avoid redundant work
PERF-109.detection_notes: Match fmt.Sprintf or custom function calls used as map index keys inside loop bodies where the arguments do not change across iterations.

PERF-110.name: sync.Pool Element Type Causes Allocation On Put
PERF-110.description: Stores non-pointer value types in sync.Pool (e.g., bytes.Buffer, not *bytes.Buffer). Each Get and Put operation boxes the value into interface{}, causing a heap allocation. Store pointer types instead to avoid the interface boxing allocation per pool operation.
PERF-110.original_description: Detect sync.Pool storing non-pointer value types causing interface boxing allocations
PERF-110.detection_notes: Match sync.Pool New functions returning non-pointer struct types. Flag if New returns a value type rather than a pointer.

PERF-111.name: Range Over String Produces Rune Allocation
PERF-111.description: Converting a string to []rune(s) before ranging allocates a full rune slice on the heap. Ranging over the string directly with for _, r := range s decodes UTF-8 incrementally without allocating. This cuts memory usage by 4x and avoids GC pressure in hot text-processing paths.
PERF-111.original_description: Detect []rune conversion of strings before range loops where direct string range avoids allocation
PERF-111.detection_notes: Match RangeStmt where the range expression is a CallExpr to []rune with a string argument.

PERF-112.name: strings.ToLower Before Comparison Instead Of EqualFold
PERF-112.description: Compares strings with strings.ToLower(s1) == strings.ToLower(s2) or similar case conversions, allocating two intermediate strings even if they differ on the first byte. strings.EqualFold compares character-by-character without allocation and returns early on mismatch, making it up to 100x faster on long strings.
PERF-112.original_description: Detect case-insensitive string comparison using ToLower/ToUpper instead of EqualFold
PERF-112.detection_notes: Match BinaryExpr with == or != where both operands are calls to strings.ToLower or strings.ToUpper.

PERF-113.name: Single-Case Select Statement Instead Of Channel Op
PERF-113.description: Uses a select statement with a single case instead of a plain channel send or receive. The select adds goroutine scheduling overhead and a runtime.selectgo call. A direct channel operation compiles to fewer instructions and communicates intent more clearly.
PERF-113.original_description: Detect select with a single case that can be replaced by a direct channel send or receive
PERF-113.detection_notes: Match SelectStmt containing exactly one CommClause with no default branch.

PERF-114.name: Manual Loop Copy Instead Of copy() Builtin
PERF-114.description: Uses a for loop to copy elements from one slice to another index-by-index instead of the copy() builtin. copy() uses optimized memory routines (often memmove in assembly) that are 3-10x faster and handle memory overlap detection.
PERF-114.original_description: Detect manual for-loop slice copying where the copy builtin would be faster
PERF-114.detection_notes: Match ForStmt with := range assigning index i and value, body containing single assignment dst[i] = val or dst[i] = src[i].

PERF-115.name: strings.Compare Used For Equality Check
PERF-115.description: Uses strings.Compare(a, b) == 0 to check string equality. strings.Compare performs a full three-way comparison before the result is checked; a direct == comparison short-circuits at the first differing byte and avoids the function call overhead entirely.
PERF-115.original_description: Detect strings.Compare used for equality or ordering where direct comparison is cheaper
PERF-115.detection_notes: Match strings.Compare calls where the return value is compared to 0 with == or !=.

PERF-116.name: strings.Index Used For Contains Check
PERF-116.description: Uses strings.Index(s, sub) != -1 to check substring containment. strings.Contains(s, sub) communicates intent more clearly and has equivalent performance, but Index comparison patterns are often vestigial from languages without a Contains function and obscure the boolean check.
PERF-116.original_description: Detect strings.Index compared to -1 where strings.Contains would be clearer
PERF-116.detection_notes: Match strings.Index calls where the return value is compared to -1.

PERF-117.name: bytes.Compare Used For Equality Check
PERF-117.description: Uses bytes.Compare(a, b) == 0 to check byte slice equality. bytes.Equal(a, b) returns a boolean directly after the first difference, short-circuiting faster than a full three-way comparison. Equal also communicates intent more clearly.
PERF-117.original_description: Detect bytes.Compare used for equality where bytes.Equal is faster and clearer
PERF-117.detection_notes: Match bytes.Compare calls where the return value is compared to 0 with == or !=.

PERF-118.name: Unnecessary http.NewRequest For Simple Methods
PERF-118.description: Uses http.NewRequest for simple GET/HEAD/POST requests when http.Get, http.Head, or http.Post would suffice. The convenience functions use DefaultClient and avoid manual request construction overhead when custom headers or body handling are not needed.
PERF-118.original_description: Detect http.NewRequest for simple methods where http.Get/Post/Head wrappers are simpler
PERF-118.detection_notes: Match http.NewRequest with method "GET", "HEAD", or "POST" without custom headers or non-standard body handling.

PERF-119.name: Multiple Separate Appends Instead Of Spread Concatenation
PERF-119.description: Uses multiple sequential append calls to the same slice instead of a single variadic append. Each separate append may trigger independent capacity growth and reallocation; combining them into append(s, a, b, c) grows once if needed, saving up to 2x allocations in element-by-element builds.
PERF-119.original_description: Detect consecutive append calls to the same slice that can be merged into one variadic call
PERF-119.detection_notes: Match 2+ sequential append calls to the same slice variable in the same block without intervening reads.

PERF-120.name: time.Now().Sub Instead Of time.Since
PERF-120.description: Uses time.Now().Sub(t) instead of time.Since(t). Both have identical behavior, but time.Since reads more naturally and avoids the explicit Now() call. When time.Now() has already been called in the function, using the cached value avoids a second syscall.
PERF-120.original_description: Detect time.Now().Sub where time.Since is clearer and avoids duplicate syscall
PERF-120.detection_notes: Match time.Now().Sub called where time.Since provides identical behavior with clearer intent.

PERF-121.name: Struct Literal Instead Of Direct Type Conversion
PERF-121.description: People often manually copy struct fields between types with identical underlying structures instead of using a direct type conversion (T(v)). The conversion is a compile-time checked zero-cost operation, while manual field copying risks bugs from missed or misordered fields and generates more code.
PERF-121.original_description: Detect manual struct field copying where direct type conversion is zero-cost
PERF-121.detection_notes: Match sequential AssignStmt nodes assigning fields from one struct to another identically-named struct with identical field types.

PERF-122.name: HasPrefix Followed By Slice Instead Of TrimPrefix
PERF-122.description: Checks strings.HasPrefix(s, prefix) and then slices s[len(prefix):] to extract the remainder. strings.TrimPrefix(s, prefix) returns the string with the prefix removed if present, or the original string otherwise, in a single call with clearer intent.
PERF-122.original_description: Detect HasPrefix + manual slice instead of TrimPrefix
PERF-122.detection_notes: Match strings.HasPrefix followed by s[len(prefix):] slice expression in the same block.

PERF-123.name: Redundant make Argument With Zero Value
PERF-123.description: Calls make([]T, 0, cap) or make(map[K]V, 0) with an explicit zero length or capacity argument. The zero value is the default; omitting it produces identical behavior with less code. For make([]T, 0), the second argument is redundant.
PERF-123.original_description: Detect make calls with explicit zero length or capacity arguments
PERF-123.detection_notes: Match make(T, 0) or make(T, 0, N) where the zero argument could be omitted.

PERF-124.name: strings.Replace With -1 Instead Of ReplaceAll
PERF-124.description: Uses strings.Replace(s, old, new, -1) with the magic number -1 to replace all occurrences. Since Go 1.12, strings.ReplaceAll(s, old, new) is available and communicates intent more clearly. Both have identical performance, but ReplaceAll avoids the magic number anti-pattern.
PERF-124.original_description: Detect strings.Replace with -1 where ReplaceAll is clearer
PERF-124.detection_notes: Match strings.Replace calls where the fourth argument is the integer literal -1.

PERF-125.name: Redundant nil Check Before append
PERF-125.description: Checks if a slice is nil before appending to it. append works correctly on nil slices (treating them as zero-length), so the nil check is unnecessary. It also adds a branch that the compiler may not optimize away.
PERF-125.original_description: Detect nil checks on slices before append where append handles nil natively
PERF-125.detection_notes: Match if s != nil blocks whose body contains append(s, ...) or s = append(s, ...).

PERF-126.name: Redundant http.CanonicalHeaderKey Call
PERF-126.description: Calls http.CanonicalHeaderKey on a header key that is already in canonical form (e.g., a constant from the http package or a previously canonicalized value). Methods like Header.Get and Header.Set already canonicalize internally, making the explicit call redundant.
PERF-126.original_description: Detect redundant http.CanonicalHeaderKey calls on already-canonical keys
PERF-126.detection_notes: Match http.CanonicalHeaderKey applied to string literals already in canonical form or values already passed through http.Header methods.

PERF-127.name: Unnecessary fmt.Sprintf In Log Call
PERF-127.description: Wraps a static string in fmt.Sprintf before passing it to a log function. The log package's Printf-style methods (log.Printf, log.Fatalf) handle formatting internally; wrapping in Sprintf first allocates an intermediate string that is immediately consumed.
PERF-127.original_description: Detect fmt.Sprintf with static string passed directly to log functions
PERF-127.detection_notes: Match log.*(fmt.Sprintf("static string without format verbs")) patterns.

PERF-128.name: Multiple Independent Appends Can Be Combined
PERF-128.description: Performs append on a slice in multiple consecutive statements when all appended values are available at once. Each separate append may independently grow the backing array. Combining them into a single variadic append reduces amortized growth overhead.
PERF-128.original_description: Detect consecutive independent append calls that can be merged
PERF-128.detection_notes: Match 3+ sequential append calls to the same slice variable without intervening reads or conditional logic.

PERF-129.name: Range Loop Copies Value When Only Index Needed
PERF-129.description: Ranges over a slice with for _, v := range and uses only the index or ignores v entirely (underscore). Even when the value is discarded, the runtime still copies each element. For large structs, the copy cost adds up. Use for i := range to iterate indices only.
PERF-129.original_description: Detect range loops that copy values when only the index is used
PERF-129.detection_notes: Match RangeStmt with key and value variables where the value variable is underscore or never read in the loop body.

PERF-130.name: Unnecessary Function Wrapper Adding Call Overhead
PERF-130.description: Wraps a function call in an anonymous function literal for no reason: func() { f(args) }(). This adds a closure allocation, indirect call overhead, and stack manipulation. If f is a named function, call it directly; if wrapping is needed for argument adaptation, use a minimal wrapper.
PERF-130.original_description: Detect unnecessary anonymous function wrappers that add call overhead
PERF-130.detection_notes: Match func() { someFunc(args) }() patterns where the wrapper provides no additional logic.

PERF-131.name: sync.Mutex Used Where sync/atomic Suffices
PERF-131.description: Uses sync.Mutex to protect a simple integer counter incremented in concurrent goroutines. atomic.AddInt64 is lock-free and significantly faster for simple arithmetic on integral types, avoiding the lock/unlock overhead and goroutine blocking.
PERF-131.original_description: Detect sync.Mutex guarding simple integer operations where atomic is lock-free
PERF-131.detection_notes: Match mu.Lock/mu.Unlock blocks where guarded operations are only simple integer increments, decrements, or reads.

PERF-132.name: Goroutine Spawned Without Context Propagation
PERF-132.description: Launches a goroutine that performs work (HTTP calls, DB queries) without receiving a context.Context from the parent. Without context propagation, the goroutine cannot be cancelled when the parent request times out or the client disconnects, leading to wasted work and goroutine leaks under load.
PERF-132.original_description: Detect goroutines doing I/O work without context propagation from parent
PERF-132.detection_notes: Match go func() without ctx parameter where the body contains HTTP, DB, or other cancellable I/O operations.

PERF-133.name: sort.Slice Closure Allocation Inside Loop
PERF-133.description: Uses sort.Slice with a closure inside a loop or repeatedly-called function, causing a heap allocation for the closure on every call. For slices of basic types (int, string, float64), sort.Ints/sort.Strings/sort.Float64s avoid the closure allocation entirely.
PERF-133.original_description: Detect sort.Slice with closure in hot paths where type-specific sort functions avoid allocation
PERF-133.detection_notes: Match sort.Slice inside loop bodies or request handlers with basic slice types where type-specific sort would work.

PERF-134.name: Manual io.Read/Write Loop Instead Of io.Copy
PERF-134.description: Implements a manual read-write loop with io.Reader and io.Writer instead of using io.Copy. io.Copy uses an internal 32KB buffer and handles EOF, short reads, and errors correctly. Manual implementations often use suboptimal buffer sizes and may mishandle edge cases.
PERF-134.original_description: Detect manual read/write loops where io.Copy is both simpler and more efficient
PERF-134.detection_notes: Match for loops containing Read and Write calls in the body where the pattern matches io.Copy semantics.

PERF-135.name: encoding/gob Encoder Or Decoder Not Reused
PERF-135.description: Creates a new gob.NewEncoder or gob.NewDecoder for every message on a persistent connection instead of reusing one encoder/decoder pair per stream. gob encoders maintain internal type info caches and buffers; reusing them avoids re-transmitting type definitions and re-allocating buffers.
PERF-135.original_description: Detect gob encoder/decoder created per message on persistent connections
PERF-135.detection_notes: Match gob.NewEncoder or gob.NewDecoder in functions containing for/range with net.Conn Read/Write patterns.

PERF-136.name: filepath.Join Repeatedly Called With Same Base
PERF-136.description: Calls filepath.Join(basePath, variant) repeatedly in a loop when the base path is constant across iterations. filepath.Join allocates a new string each call. Compute the joined path once by pre-joining with a placeholder or use direct string concatenation if the base is already clean.
PERF-136.original_description: Detect filepath.Join with constant base path called repeatedly in loops
PERF-136.detection_notes: Match filepath.Join inside loop bodies where the first argument is a string literal or loop-invariant variable.

PERF-137.name: runtime.Caller Used In Hot Path
PERF-137.description: Calls runtime.Caller on hot paths to determine the calling function. runtime.Caller walks the stack, which involves reading stack frames and program counter tables. On hot paths this adds measurable overhead; cache the result or use a compile-time constant where possible.
PERF-137.original_description: Detect runtime.Caller in request handlers or hot loops where it adds stack-walking overhead
PERF-137.detection_notes: Match runtime.Caller call expressions inside request handlers or for/range loop bodies.

PERF-138.name: runtime.Stack Used In Hot Path
PERF-138.description: Calls runtime.Stack on hot paths to capture goroutine stack traces. This allocates a buffer and formats the stack, which is expensive (hundreds of microseconds). Stack traces should be captured only for error reporting or debugging, not on production hot paths.
PERF-138.original_description: Detect runtime.Stack in request handlers where stack trace formatting adds latency
PERF-138.detection_notes: Match runtime.Stack call expressions inside request handlers or production code paths.

PERF-139.name: Closure Allocates Due To Variable Escape
PERF-139.description: A closure captures multiple large outer variables, causing each to escape to the heap as separate allocations. Grouping captured variables into a single struct reduces N allocations to 1, since the struct itself escapes but its fields are stored contiguously.
PERF-139.original_description: Detect closures capturing multiple large variables causing separate heap allocations
PERF-139.detection_notes: Match func() literals inside loops or hot functions referencing 2+ outer-scope variables of non-trivial size.

PERF-140.name: debug.SetGCPercent Misuse Or Tuning In Production
PERF-140.description: Calls debug.SetGCPercent with aggressive values (very low or -1 to disable) in production code without corresponding GOMEMLIMIT. Setting GCPercent too low increases GC frequency and CPU usage; setting it to -1 disables GC entirely and risks OOM without a memory limit backstop.
PERF-140.original_description: Detect debug.SetGCPercent calls in production code paths without memory limit safeguards
PERF-140.detection_notes: Match debug.SetGCPercent call expressions; flag when value set below 50 or to -1 without nearby GOMEMLIMIT configuration.

PERF-141.name: URL.Query() Called Repeatedly Without Caching
PERF-141.description: Calls r.URL.Query() or r.URL.Query().Get(key) multiple times in a single handler. Each call to URL.Query() parses the raw query string and allocates a new url.Values map. Cache the result in a local variable to parse once and reuse across the handler.
PERF-141.original_description: Detect repeated calls to URL.Query() in the same handler without caching the parsed values
PERF-141.detection_notes: Match 2+ r.URL.Query().Get or r.URL.Query() calls on the same *http.Request in one handler function.

PERF-142.name: http.MaxBytesReader Not Used For Untrusted Body
PERF-142.description: Reads from an HTTP request body (r.Body) without wrapping it in http.MaxBytesReader. A malicious client can send unbounded data, causing the server to allocate memory until OOM. http.MaxBytesReader limits the request body to a configured maximum and returns an error on overflow.
PERF-142.original_description: Detect request body reads without MaxBytesReader protection against unbounded input
PERF-142.detection_notes: Match io.ReadAll(r.Body) or json.NewDecoder(r.Body) without a preceding http.MaxBytesReader wrapper.

PERF-143.name: http.TimeoutHandler Not Used For Route-Level Timeouts
PERF-143.description: Exposes HTTP endpoints without per-route timeout enforcement via http.TimeoutHandler, relying solely on the server-level timeouts. Server-level timeouts apply to the entire request lifecycle; per-route timeouts allow different deadlines for fast endpoints vs slow ones.
PERF-143.original_description: Detect route handlers without TimeoutHandler wrapping where per-route timeouts would improve resilience
PERF-143.detection_notes: Match mux.Handle or mux.HandleFunc calls without surrounding http.TimeoutHandler in the handler chain.

PERF-144.name: Content-Length Not Set In HTTP Response
PERF-144.description: Serves HTTP responses with known body sizes but does not set Content-Length header. Without Content-Length, Go uses chunked transfer encoding, which adds per-chunk overhead. Setting Content-Length enables the client to show progress bars and allows connection reuse optimizations.
PERF-144.original_description: Detect HTTP responses with known body size missing Content-Length header
PERF-144.detection_notes: Match w.Write calls in handlers where the response body size is known from a byte slice and Content-Length is not set before Write.

PERF-145.name: http.Request.WithContext Allocation On Hot Path
