PERF-145.description: Calls r.WithContext(ctx) in middleware or request chains, which shallow-copies the entire http.Request struct (~2KB). Each WithContext call in the chain allocates a fresh copy. Prefer passing context values through a separate mechanism or using r.Clone(ctx) only at boundaries.
PERF-145.original_description: Detect r.WithContext called repeatedly in middleware chains causing per-request allocations
PERF-145.detection_notes: Match r.WithContext in functions named middleware or registered via Use/Group in multiple chain layers.

PERF-146.name: fmt.Sprintf With Single String And No Verbs
PERF-146.description: Uses fmt.Sprintf("%s", s) where s is already a string. This parses the format string, builds an argument list, and copies s to a new string — but s is already a string. The fmt overhead is 20-50x more expensive than using s directly.
PERF-146.original_description: Detect fmt.Sprintf with literal format containing only "%s" and a single string argument
PERF-146.detection_notes: Match fmt.Sprintf calls where the first argument is "%s" and there is exactly one additional argument of type string.

PERF-147.name: strings.Replace Call Where ReplaceAll Suffices
PERF-147.description: Uses strings.Replace with a negative count to replace all occurrences, where strings.ReplaceAll communicates the intent more clearly. While performance is identical, the magic-number pattern obscures the 'replace all' intention and is a common source of bugs when the count is accidentally set to 0 or 1.
PERF-147.original_description: Detect strings.Replace with negative count where ReplaceAll is cleaner
PERF-147.detection_notes: Match strings.Replace calls where the fourth argument is a negative integer literal.

PERF-148.name: Goroutine Leak Via Channel Send Without Guaranteed Receiver
PERF-148.description: Sends to an unbuffered channel from a goroutine without guaranteeing a corresponding receive on all code paths. If the receiver exits early via error return or context cancellation, the sender blocks forever on the unbuffered channel, leaking the goroutine and its stack memory.
PERF-148.original_description: Detect unbuffered channel sends in goroutines without guaranteed receive on all exit paths
PERF-148.detection_notes: Match ch <- value inside goroutine where the receive is guarded by select with default or conditional logic that may skip.

PERF-149.name: net.Conn Deadlines Not Set For Network Operations
PERF-149.description: Performs network I/O (Read, Write) on net.Conn without setting deadlines. Without deadlines, a stalled peer can block the goroutine indefinitely. SetReadDeadline and SetWriteDeadline ensure I/O operations time out rather than hanging.
PERF-149.original_description: Detect network Read/Write operations without configured deadlines
PERF-149.detection_notes: Match conn.Read or conn.Write calls where SetReadDeadline or SetWriteDeadline is not called on the same connection in the function or its caller.

PERF-150.name: Large Stack Frame From Local Variables
PERF-150.description: Declares large arrays or many local variables that exceed the compiler's stack frame threshold, preventing the function from being inlined. Functions that cannot be inlined add call overhead at every invocation. Split into a small fast-path inlinable function and a slow-path non-inlinable function.
PERF-150.original_description: Detect functions with large stack frames that prevent compiler inlining on hot paths
PERF-150.detection_notes: Find functions exceeding ~40 lines called from hot paths or loops. Confirm inlining with go build -gcflags="-m" analysis.

PERF-151.name: Non-Inlinable Function On Hot Path Due To Complexity
PERF-151.description: A hot-path function exceeds the Go compiler's inlining budget (~80 cost units as of Go 1.22). Each defer, for loop, and select adds to the budget. Functions that cross the threshold add ~5-15ns call overhead per invocation. Split hot fast-path logic from cold slow-path logic.
PERF-151.original_description: Detect hot-path functions exceeding compiler inlining budget due to complexity
PERF-151.detection_notes: Match functions >40 lines called from hot paths. Confirm with go build -gcflags="-m". Estimate cost by counting AST nodes.

PERF-152.name: Header Copy Via Manual Loop Instead Of Clone
PERF-152.description: Copies HTTP headers by iterating over the source header map with a for-range loop and setting each key individually, allocating new string slices per header. http.Header.Clone() does a shallow copy in bulk and is significantly faster for header forwarding patterns.
PERF-152.original_description: Detect manual header copying loops where Header.Clone is more efficient
PERF-152.detection_notes: Match for k, v := range over Header variable with header.Add or header.Set in the loop body.

PERF-153.name: http.Cookie.String Called Repeatedly
PERF-153.description: Calls http.Cookie.String() multiple times for the same cookie in a request handler. The String method re-serializes the cookie (including name-value encoding and attribute formatting) each call. Cache the serialized string or extract only the needed field.
PERF-153.original_description: Detect repeated http.Cookie.String calls on the same cookie in one handler
PERF-153.detection_notes: Match cookie.String() called 2+ times on the same *http.Cookie variable in a single function scope.

PERF-154.name: Unnecessary http.HandlerFunc Type Conversion
PERF-154.description: Converts a function matching the http.HandlerFunc signature to http.HandlerFunc type and then passes it to http.Handle. Both Handle and HandleFunc accept compatible function types; the explicit http.HandlerFunc conversion is a no-op that adds syntactic noise.
PERF-154.detection_notes: Match http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { ... }) conversion patterns.

PERF-155.name: http.ServeMux Pattern Without Method Restriction
PERF-155.description: Registers route handlers that internally check r.Method with if/switch statements instead of using method-aware mux patterns or Go 1.22+ method routing. This duplicates HTTP method dispatching logic and makes the handler responsible for method validation that the mux should handle.
PERF-155.original_description: Detect handlers with inline HTTP method checks where mux-level method routing would work
PERF-155.detection_notes: Match if r.Method == ... or switch r.Method inside handler functions registered via Handle or HandleFunc.

PERF-156.name: Ranging Over String With Only Index Usage
PERF-156.description: Ranges over a string with for i, ch := range s when only the index i is used (ch is underscore). The runtime still decodes UTF-8 runes into ch on every iteration. Use a standard for i := 0; i < len(s); i++ loop to iterate bytes without the rune decoding overhead.
PERF-156.original_description: Detect string range loops decoding runes when only byte indices are needed
PERF-156.detection_notes: Match for i, _ := range str patterns and for _, r := range patterns where the rune value is unused.

PERF-157.name: Unnecessary Use Of fmt.Sprint With Single String
PERF-157.description: Calls fmt.Sprint(x) where x is already a string. fmt.Sprint parses the format, boxes arguments, and copies the result, returning x unchanged. The call is 100x+ overhead compared to using x directly. Similarly fmt.Sprintln adds an unnecessary newline.
PERF-157.original_description: Detect fmt.Sprint or fmt.Sprintln called with a single string argument
PERF-157.detection_notes: Match fmt.Sprint or fmt.Sprintln calls with exactly one argument whose type is string.

PERF-158.name: Sorting Slice Of Basic Types With Closure
PERF-158.description: Uses sort.Slice with a closure to sort a slice of basic types (int, string, float64) instead of using the type-specific sort.Ints, sort.Strings, or sort.Float64s. sort.Slice allocates a closure per call; the type-specific functions are closure-free and slightly faster.
PERF-158.original_description: Detect sort.Slice on basic-type slices where type-specific sort functions avoid closure allocation
PERF-158.detection_notes: Match sort.Slice calls with []int, []string, []float64, []int64, []uint64 where comparison is simple < or >.

PERF-159.name: Using encoding/json RawMessage Instead Of json.Decoder For Streaming
PERF-159.description: Uses json.Unmarshal with io.ReadAll to parse JSON from a reader, buffering the entire body before deserialization. json.NewDecoder(r).Decode(&v) streams directly from the reader, avoiding the intermediate byte slice allocation and enabling partial/streaming JSON processing.
PERF-159.original_description: Detect io.ReadAll + json.Unmarshal pattern where NewDecoder streaming is more efficient
PERF-159.detection_notes: Match io.ReadAll or ioutil.ReadAll immediately followed by json.Unmarshal on the same data.

PERF-160.name: sql.Open Inside Request Handler
PERF-160.description: Calls sql.Open inside a request handler or loop, creating a new connection pool per invocation instead of sharing a single *sql.DB singleton created once at startup. Each sql.Open creates new connections, bypasses pool tuning, and leaks idle connections under load.
PERF-160.original_description: Detect sql.Open called inside handlers or hot paths instead of once at startup
PERF-160.detection_notes: Match sql.Open call expressions inside functions with request-handler signatures or inside loop bodies.

PERF-161.name: rows.Err Not Checked After Iteration
PERF-161.description: Iterates over sql.Rows with for rows.Next() but never checks rows.Err() after the loop exits. This silently drops scan errors and row-level failures, potentially missing database errors that occurred mid-iteration and leaking database cursors.
PERF-161.original_description: Detect rows.Next() loops missing rows.Err() check after the loop body
PERF-161.detection_notes: Match for rows.Next() patterns and verify rows.Err() is called after the closing brace of the loop.

PERF-162.name: db.Ping Inside Request Handler
PERF-162.description: Calls db.Ping or db.PingContext on every request to verify database liveness, adding an unnecessary round-trip per inbound call. A health-check endpoint or separate goroutine with periodic pings avoids this per-request database overhead.
PERF-162.original_description: Detect db.Ping inside handlers or middleware in request-serving hot paths
PERF-162.detection_notes: Match db.Ping or db.PingContext call expressions inside request-handler function bodies.

PERF-163.name: db.Query Instead Of QueryRow For Single Row
PERF-163.description: Uses db.Query or db.QueryContext for queries that return at most one row, then iterates with rows.Next() and rows.Scan(). db.QueryRow or db.QueryRowContext avoids the rowset iteration overhead and shortens the code for single-row queries.
PERF-163.original_description: Detect db.Query followed by single rows.Next() where QueryRow suffices
PERF-163.detection_notes: Match db.Query expressions consumed in a block calling rows.Next() exactly once without a loop.

PERF-164.name: Missing Context In Database Calls
PERF-164.description: Uses database/sql methods like db.Query, db.Exec, or db.Prepare without their Context variants in request-serving code. The non-context variants lose cancellation propagation and request-level timeout enforcement, leading to queries that continue executing after the client disconnects.
PERF-164.original_description: Detect db.Query/Exec/Prepare without Context suffix in request handlers where context is available
PERF-164.detection_notes: Match bare db.Query/Exec/Prepare calls inside functions with a ctx parameter where the Context variant is not used.

PERF-165.name: Not Implementing sql.Scanner For Custom Types
PERF-165.description: Scans database values into intermediate string or []byte variables and then parses them into a custom type, instead of implementing the sql.Scanner interface on the custom type. The Scanner interface avoids the per-row intermediate allocation and parse step.
PERF-165.original_description: Detect rows.Scan into temp variables followed by conversion where sql.Scanner avoids the intermediate
PERF-165.detection_notes: Match rows.Scan into a primitive type followed by assignment or conversion to a custom type on the next line.

PERF-166.name: database/sql Null Handling Without sql.Null Types
PERF-166.description: Scans nullable database columns into pointer types (*string, *int) and dereferences them on every row. The sql.NullString, sql.NullInt64, and similar types avoid heap-escaping the pointed-to values and provide explicit Valid fields for null checking.
PERF-166.original_description: Detect *string or *int used in rows.Scan where sql.Null types avoid per-row allocation
PERF-166.detection_notes: Match rows.Scan with pointer-type destination arguments where the pointer value is dereferenced for null checking.

PERF-167.name: WaitGroup.Add Inside Goroutine
PERF-167.description: Calls wg.Add(1) inside the goroutine body instead of before the go statement. This creates a race condition where wg.Wait() can return before Add is executed, causing zero or negative WaitGroup counters and premature Wait returns.
PERF-167.original_description: Detect sync.WaitGroup.Add inside goroutine instead of before the go statement
PERF-167.detection_notes: Match wg.Add inside goroutine literal body; verify Add appeared before the enclosing go statement.

PERF-168.name: Large Struct Sent By Value Over Channel
PERF-168.description: Sends large structs by value through channels instead of using pointers. Each channel operation copies the full struct; for structs with many fields, strings, or slices, the copy cost plus potential escape analysis overhead can be significant under throughput.
PERF-168.original_description: Detect channel sends of large struct values where pointers would avoid copying
PERF-168.detection_notes: Match ch <- structValue where the struct type has more than ~3 fields or contains slice/map/string fields.

PERF-169.name: atomic.Value Frequent Store Allocation
PERF-169.description: Uses sync/atomic.Value for values frequently updated in hot paths. Each Store boxes the value into an interface{}, allocating on the heap. For frequently updated counters or pointers, typed atomic types (atomic.Int64, atomic.Pointer) or a mutex-protected value are cheaper.
PERF-169.original_description: Detect atomic.Value.Store called repeatedly in hot paths where typed atomics avoid interface boxing
PERF-169.detection_notes: Match atomic.Value declarations where .Store is called inside a loop or request handler.

PERF-170.name: sync.Once In Hot Function Path
PERF-170.description: Places sync.Once.Do() inside a function called on every request or in a loop, adding an atomic check and fast-path overhead even after initialization completes. Move the sync.Once to package level or hoist it so the hot path never touches it after the first call.
PERF-170.original_description: Detect sync.Once.Do in request handlers where initialization belongs at startup
PERF-170.detection_notes: Match sync.Once.Do calls inside functions that are request handlers, middleware, or called within loop bodies.

PERF-171.name: Channel Used As Mutex
PERF-171.description: Uses a buffered channel with capacity 1 in a send-to-acquire, receive-to-release pattern for mutual exclusion instead of sync.Mutex. Channel-based mutual exclusion adds goroutine scheduling overhead and is harder to make deadlock-free compared to mutex-based locking.
PERF-171.original_description: Detect channel used as mutual exclusion lock where sync.Mutex would be simpler and faster
PERF-171.detection_notes: Match make(chan struct{}, 1) or make(chan bool, 1) where send and receive are used for acquire/release with no data transfer.

PERF-172.name: WaitGroup.Wait Blocking Serving Goroutine
PERF-172.description: Calls sync.WaitGroup.Wait() in a request handler to wait for spawned goroutines, blocking the serving goroutine until all sub-tasks complete. This ties up a goroutine that could be serving other requests; use a result channel or errgroup with context for better resource utilization.
PERF-172.original_description: Detect wg.Wait in request handlers blocking the serving goroutine
PERF-172.detection_notes: Match wg.Wait calls inside functions with request-handler signatures without worker pool delegation.

PERF-173.name: time.Tick Not Stopped Causing Goroutine Leak
PERF-173.description: Uses time.NewTicker in a long-running function without calling ticker.Stop(). The underlying timer goroutine continues firing even after the ticker variable goes out of scope, leaking a goroutine for the lifetime of the process. Always defer ticker.Stop().
PERF-173.original_description: Detect time.NewTicker without corresponding Stop call when ticker leaves scope
PERF-173.detection_notes: Match time.NewTicker where Stop() is not called in any reachable code path before the ticker variable goes out of scope.

PERF-174.name: Closing Channel By Receiver
PERF-174.description: Closes a channel from the receiver side rather than the sender. If the sender tries to send after the close, it panics with 'send on closed channel'. The sender should own the channel lifecycle; the receiver should signal completion via a separate done channel or context cancellation.
PERF-174.original_description: Detect close(ch) where the enclosing goroutine primarily receives rather than sends
PERF-174.detection_notes: Match close(ch) inside a function that contains more <-ch receive operations than ch <- send operations.

PERF-175.name: Buffered Channel Spinning On Receive
PERF-175.description: Tight-loops on receiving from a buffered channel without a select or context deadline, consuming CPU when the channel is empty. A select with a default case and small sleep, or a blocking receive with context cancellation, avoids the CPU spin.
PERF-175.original_description: Detect tight-loop receives from buffered channels without backoff or cancellation
PERF-175.detection_notes: Match for { <-ch } or looped receives without select, context, or backoff mechanisms.

PERF-176.name: io.Copy Without Buffer Reuse
PERF-176.description: Uses io.Copy without providing a reusable buffer via io.CopyBuffer. io.Copy allocates a 32KB buffer internally on every call. When copying in a loop or per-request, providing a shared buffer via io.CopyBuffer avoids this repeated allocation.
PERF-176.original_description: Detect io.Copy in hot paths without io.CopyBuffer with a pre-allocated reusable buffer
PERF-176.detection_notes: Match io.Copy inside loop bodies or request handlers; check if io.CopyBuffer with a pooled buffer is used.

PERF-177.name: os.File.Readdir Instead Of os.ReadDir
PERF-177.description: Uses (*os.File).Readdir to list directory entries, which returns []os.FileInfo and stats every entry. os.ReadDir (Go 1.16+) returns []os.DirEntry, deferring the stat call. For use cases that only need names or types, ReadDir avoids unnecessary filesystem syscalls per entry.
PERF-177.original_description: Detect os.File.Readdir where os.ReadDir avoids per-entry stat syscalls
PERF-177.detection_notes: Match .Readdir() method calls on *os.File values; suppress when FileInfo is explicitly required.

PERF-178.name: time.Format Instead Of time.AppendFormat
PERF-178.description: Uses time.Format which allocates a new string on every call. When formatting many timestamps in a loop, time.AppendFormat with a reusable byte slice avoids per-call string allocation, reducing GC pressure for high-throughput timestamp formatting.
PERF-178.original_description: Detect time.Format called repeatedly in loops where AppendFormat avoids allocation
PERF-178.detection_notes: Match t.Format(layout) inside loop bodies where the result is appended to a byte buffer.

PERF-179.name: strings.Replacer Not Used For Repeated Replace
PERF-179.description: Calls strings.Replace or strings.ReplaceAll repeatedly in a loop with the same old/new pairs on different inputs. strings.NewReplacer compiles the replacement set once and amortizes the cost across calls, reducing per-iteration parsing overhead.
PERF-179.original_description: Detect repeated strings.Replace/ReplaceAll with same pairs in loops where NewReplacer is cheaper
PERF-179.detection_notes: Match strings.Replace or strings.ReplaceAll with string literal arguments inside loop bodies.

PERF-180.name: encoding/csv Reader Per Row
PERF-180.description: Creates a new csv.NewReader for each row or batch in a hot parsing path instead of reusing one reader instance. Each csv.NewReader allocates internal buffers; reusing the reader across reads from the same underlying io.Reader avoids repeated allocation.
PERF-180.original_description: Detect csv.NewReader created per-row in loops where reuse avoids repeated allocation
PERF-180.detection_notes: Match csv.NewReader inside loop bodies or handler functions where the reader could be created once.

PERF-181.name: json.Decoder UseNumber Missing
PERF-181.description: Uses json.NewDecoder without calling .UseNumber() when decoded JSON contains numeric values that will be immediately used as integers. Without UseNumber(), the decoder parses all numbers as float64, requiring a lossy float-to-int conversion per numeric field.
PERF-181.original_description: Detect json.NewDecoder without UseNumber where decoded struct has int fields
PERF-181.detection_notes: Match json.NewDecoder without following .UseNumber() when the decoded struct has int/int64 fields.

PERF-182.name: bufio.Writer Default Buffer Undersized
PERF-182.description: Uses bufio.NewWriter with the default 4096-byte buffer when typical writes are significantly larger. This causes excessive flush operations and small syscalls. Setting a buffer size matching expected write chunks with bufio.NewWriterSize reduces syscall overhead.
PERF-182.original_description: Detect bufio.NewWriter with default buffer when write sizes exceed 4096 bytes
PERF-182.detection_notes: Match bufio.NewWriter(w) without explicit size where subsequent Write calls pass large byte slices.

PERF-183.name: context.WithTimeout Inside Loop
PERF-183.description: Creates a new context.WithTimeout or context.WithCancel inside a loop body, allocating a new timer and context value chain on every iteration. Hoist the context derivation outside the loop or use a single long-lived context with per-iteration deadline checks.
PERF-183.original_description: Detect context.WithTimeout or context.WithCancel inside loop bodies
PERF-183.detection_notes: Match context.WithTimeout or context.WithCancel call expressions inside for/range loop bodies.

PERF-184.name: mime.TypeByExtension In Hot Path
PERF-184.description: Calls mime.TypeByExtension in request-serving paths to determine Content-Type headers. This function performs a map lookup and string manipulation per call. Cache the result or use a constant map for the few MIME types your application serves.
PERF-184.original_description: Detect mime.TypeByExtension called inside request handlers where caching avoids repeated lookup
PERF-184.detection_notes: Match mime.TypeByExtension call expressions inside handler or middleware functions.

PERF-185.name: http.DetectContentType In Request Handler
PERF-185.description: Uses http.DetectContentType in request-serving handlers to sniff file content types. This function reads up to 512 bytes and performs pattern matching on every call. Cache the detected type or use explicit MIME type mappings for known file extensions.
PERF-185.original_description: Detect http.DetectContentType called inside request handlers or middleware
PERF-185.detection_notes: Match http.DetectContentType call expressions inside handler or middleware functions.

PERF-186.name: strings.Fields In Hot Parsing Path
PERF-186.description: Uses strings.Fields or strings.FieldsFunc in hot parsing paths, allocating a slice of all substrings at once. For large inputs or high-throughput paths, a bufio.Scanner or manual index-based parsing avoids allocating all fields simultaneously.
PERF-186.original_description: Detect strings.Fields in hot parsing paths where streaming parsing reduces allocation
PERF-186.detection_notes: Match strings.Fields or strings.FieldsFunc inside loop bodies or handler functions on potentially large inputs.

PERF-187.name: template.HTMLEscaper In Hot Path
PERF-187.description: Calls template.HTMLEscaper or template.JSEscaper repeatedly in hot paths that build HTML or JavaScript fragments. These functions allocate a new string per call. Pre-escaping at startup or using html/template's automatic contextual escaping is more efficient.
PERF-187.original_description: Detect template.HTMLEscaper or template.JSEscaper in loops or request-handler hot paths
PERF-187.detection_notes: Match template.HTMLEscaper or template.JSEscaper inside loop bodies or handler functions.

PERF-188.name: fmt.Sscanf In Hot Path
PERF-188.description: Uses fmt.Sscanf or fmt.Sscanf in performance-sensitive parsing paths. These functions use reflection to parse according to format strings and are orders of magnitude slower than direct strconv or manual parsing with strings.Index and strconv.Atoi.
PERF-188.original_description: Detect fmt.Sscanf or fmt.Sscanf inside loop bodies or request handlers
PERF-188.detection_notes: Match fmt.Sscanf or fmt.Sscanf with a known format string inside loop bodies or handler functions.

PERF-189.name: HTTP Response Body Not Drained Before Close
PERF-189.description: Calls resp.Body.Close() without fully consuming the response body first. HTTP/1.1 persistent connections cannot be reused unless the entire body is read. This causes a new TCP connection and TLS handshake for every request instead of reusing idle connections from the transport pool.
PERF-189.original_description: Detect resp.Body.Close() without preceding body drain when response has unread data
PERF-189.detection_notes: Match defer resp.Body.Close() where resp is *http.Response; check if body is fully consumed before Close.

PERF-190.name: HTTP Client Missing Timeout
PERF-190.description: Creates an http.Client without setting the Timeout field, leaving outbound HTTP requests susceptible to hanging indefinitely. A hung request ties up a goroutine, and under load this cascade-exhausts goroutines and memory. Always set an explicit Timeout on production HTTP clients.
PERF-190.original_description: Detect http.Client without Timeout field for outbound requests
PERF-190.detection_notes: Match http.Client composite literals that do not set the Timeout field; check if context deadline is always provided on Do calls.

PERF-191.name: Slice Of Pointers For Small Structs
PERF-191.description: Uses []*SmallStruct when []SmallStruct would provide better cache locality and avoid pointer-chasing overhead. Each pointer dereference causes a cache miss when iterating, and the pointed-to values are individually heap-allocated and GC-scanned rather than stored contiguously.
PERF-191.original_description: Detect slice of pointers to small structs where value slices improve cache locality
PERF-191.detection_notes: Match []*T declarations where T is a small struct (few fields, no mutex) and the slice is ranged in hot paths.

PERF-192.name: Map Without Size Hint
PERF-192.description: Creates a map with make(map[K]V) without a capacity hint when the approximate number of entries is known at allocation time. Without a hint, the map must resize its hash table multiple times as entries are added, causing rehashing and allocation churn.
PERF-192.original_description: Detect make(map[K]V) without capacity hint when eventual size can be determined
PERF-192.detection_notes: Match make(map[K]V) without second argument where the map is populated from a slice with known length.

PERF-193.name: Not Resetting Timer In Loop
PERF-193.description: Creates a new time.NewTimer on every loop iteration instead of calling .Reset() on an existing timer. Even though Go 1.23+ improved Reset safety, creating a new timer per iteration allocates and registers a new runtime timer object, adding GC pressure.
PERF-193.original_description: Detect time.NewTimer created inside loop where an existing timer could be Reset
PERF-193.detection_notes: Match time.NewTimer(d) inside for loop bodies; suppress when duration varies per iteration.

PERF-194.name: Using time.Sleep For Polling
PERF-194.description: Uses time.Sleep in a for loop as a polling mechanism instead of event-driven patterns like time.Ticker, fsnotify, or channel-based notifications. Sleep-based polling wastes CPU on wake-ups and adds latency equal to the sleep interval.
PERF-194.original_description: Detect time.Sleep in loops used as polling where event-driven patterns avoid wake-up overhead
PERF-194.detection_notes: Match time.Sleep(d) inside an infinite or conditional for loop without select or channel wake-up operations.

PERF-195.name: log.Fatal Or log.Panic In Goroutine
PERF-195.description: Uses log.Fatal or log.Panic inside a goroutine that is not the main goroutine. log.Fatal calls os.Exit(1), terminating the entire process without allowing other goroutines to clean up. log.Panic unwinds only the calling goroutine, leaving others in an undefined state.
PERF-195.original_description: Detect log.Fatal or log.Panic inside goroutines launched from handlers or workers
PERF-195.detection_notes: Match log.Fatal, log.Fatalf, log.Fatalln, log.Panic inside go func() literal bodies or goroutine-launched functions.

PERF-196.name: JWT Token Parsing Per Handler
PERF-196.description: Parses and validates a JWT token in individual route handlers instead of in a shared authentication middleware. This duplicates cryptographic signature verification work (RSA/ECDSA/HMAC operations) on every handler in the chain.
PERF-196.original_description: Detect JWT parsing called inside individual route handlers rather than in middleware
PERF-196.detection_notes: Match JWT Parse/ParseWithClaims calls inside route handler functions; suppress when middleware already validates.

PERF-197.name: Multiple io.ReadAll On Request Body
PERF-197.description: Reads the HTTP request body with io.ReadAll or json.NewDecoder in multiple places (middleware then handler). The body is a read-once stream; the second read gets EOF. Re-reading requires restoring the body via io.NopCloser with a bytes.Buffer, doubling memory for the body.
PERF-197.original_description: Detect multiple body reads on the same http.Request without restoration
PERF-197.detection_notes: Match 2+ body-read calls on the same *http.Request in the same function or across middleware-handler chains.

PERF-198.name: Content-Type Check With strings.Contains
PERF-198.description: Checks the Content-Type header using strings.Contains(header, "json") instead of a direct comparison or switch. strings.Contains performs a full substring scan and may false-match (e.g., "application/vnd.api+json" matches both "json" and "xml").
PERF-198.original_description: Detect strings.Contains on Content-Type headers where precise comparison would be correct
PERF-198.detection_notes: Match strings.Contains on Content-Type header values where a precise check would be faster and correct.

PERF-199.name: Session Store Lookup Per Handler
PERF-199.description: Looks up the user session from a session store (Redis, database, cookie) on every individual route handler, instead of extracting it once in middleware. Under load with a remote session store, this multiplies network round-trips by the number of handlers in the chain.
PERF-199.original_description: Detect session store lookups inside individual route handlers rather than in middleware
PERF-199.detection_notes: Match session store Get/Start calls inside route handler functions.

PERF-200.name: Middleware Ordering Penalty
PERF-200.description: Places expensive middleware (authentication, body parsing, rate limiting) before cheap early-reject middleware (CORS preflight, request size check, IP allowlist). An early-rejected request still pays the cost of the expensive middleware, wasting CPU on rejected requests.
PERF-200.original_description: Detect middleware chains where expensive work appears before cheap rejection middleware
PERF-200.detection_notes: Match router.Use or Group.Use call sequences; check if CORS/request-ID/size-limit middleware appear after auth/body-parsing/rate-limiting.

PERF-201.name: CORS Preflight Handler Allocation
PERF-201.description: Implements a custom CORS preflight handler that allocates response headers or performs work for OPTIONS requests. Using a well-known CORS middleware (rs/cors, gin-contrib/cors) that short-circuits on preflight is cheaper, well-tested, and avoids custom allocation overhead.
PERF-201.original_description: Detect custom OPTIONS handlers with header manipulation where standard CORS middleware is cheaper
PERF-201.detection_notes: Match custom OPTIONS route handlers setting Access-Control-* headers; check if a standard CORS middleware is imported.

PERF-202.name: json.Marshal Indent In Production Handler
PERF-202.description: Uses json.MarshalIndent or json.Encoder.SetIndent in production handlers to pretty-print JSON responses. Indentation adds whitespace (increasing response size) and requires extra allocation for the formatted output. Pretty-printing should be development-only or gated by a debug flag.
PERF-202.original_description: Detect json.MarshalIndent or SetIndent used in request handlers where compact output suffices
PERF-202.detection_notes: Match json.MarshalIndent or encoder.SetIndent inside handler functions or middleware.

PERF-203.name: net.IP.String Repeated In Hot Path
PERF-203.description: Calls net.IP.String() repeatedly on the same IP address in hot paths. Each call allocates a new string for the string representation. Cache the string form once or use a formatting helper that writes to a reusable buffer.
PERF-203.original_description: Detect repeated net.IP.String calls on same IP in handlers where caching avoids allocation
PERF-203.detection_notes: Match ip.String() called 2+ times in the same function scope with the same IP variable.

PERF-204.name: GORM Updates With Map Without Select
PERF-204.description: Uses db.Model(&record).Updates(map[string]interface{}{...}) without a preceding .Select() clause. This updates all fields in the map unconditionally, including zero-value fields, potentially overwriting data and generating larger UPDATE statements than needed.
PERF-204.original_description: Detect GORM Updates with map argument without Select to restrict updated columns
PERF-204.detection_notes: Match db.Updates(map[...]) or db.Model().Updates(map[...]) without .Select preceding the call.

PERF-205.name: GORM Pagination Without Count Optimization
PERF-205.description: Calls db.Count(&total) followed by db.Offset(offset).Limit(limit).Find(&records) on every paginated list request, performing two queries per page. For large tables, OFFSET-based pagination degrades linearly; keyset pagination avoids the performance cliff.
PERF-205.original_description: Detect GORM Count + Offset + Limit pattern where keyset pagination avoids OFFSET performance degradation
PERF-205.detection_notes: Match db.Count followed by db.Offset().Limit().Find in the same handler or function.

PERF-206.name: sqlx Unsafe Without Known Input
PERF-206.description: Uses sqlx.Unsafe to disable compile-time SQL name binding resolution when the query string is built from runtime input. Unsafe mode should only be used with static queries; dynamic queries with unsafe lose correctness and performance optimizations.
PERF-206.original_description: Detect sqlx.Unsafe mode used with runtime-built queries where static queries should be used
PERF-206.detection_notes: Match sqlx.Unsafe or db.Unsafe() usage; check if the query argument is built from variables rather than a string literal.

PERF-207.name: Fiber ctx.SendFile Without Caching
PERF-207.description: Uses c.SendFile in Fiber handlers without setting cache headers (Cache-Control, ETag, Last-Modified). Each request re-reads and re-transmits the file, wasting disk I/O and network bandwidth for static assets that rarely change.
PERF-207.original_description: Detect Fiber SendFile without cache headers set for static assets
PERF-207.detection_notes: Match c.SendFile in Fiber route handlers without preceding cache header middleware or manual header setup.

PERF-208.name: Prometheus Counter Without Bounded Label Set
PERF-208.description: Creates a Prometheus counter or gauge vector with a label dimension that includes unbounded values (URL path with IDs, user emails, full URLs). This causes unbounded time series growth, exhausting Prometheus client memory and remote storage over time.
PERF-208.original_description: Detect Prometheus metrics with unbounded label values from user input or IDs
PERF-208.detection_notes: Match NewCounterVec/NewHistogramVec with label names containing id, user, url, email, token; check if values from path params.

PERF-209.name: Cobra PersistentPreRun In Every Command
PERF-209.description: Defines heavy PersistentPreRun or PersistentPostRun hooks on parent Cobra commands that execute for every subcommand invocation, even when the subcommand does not need the setup work. Use subcommand-specific hooks or lazy initialization for expensive parent-command hooks.
PERF-209.original_description: Detect expensive Cobra PersistentPreRun hooks running for every subcommand
PERF-209.detection_notes: Match PersistentPreRunE/PersistentPostRunE on parent commands; check if hook body contains heavy init or network calls.

PERF-210.name: go-redis KEYS Command In Application Code
PERF-210.description: Uses the Redis KEYS command via go-redis in application logic, which scans the entire keyspace and blocks the Redis server synchronously. For production use, SCAN (rdb.Scan) iterates incrementally without blocking, at the cost of slightly more complex pagination logic.
PERF-210.original_description: Detect go-redis KEYS command in application handler paths where SCAN avoids server blocking
PERF-210.detection_notes: Match .Keys( call on go-redis client types inside handler, middleware, or application logic functions.

PERF-211.name: GORM Not In Select Clause
PERF-211.description: Uses GORM's Not() condition on indexed queries where NOT IN, NOT LIKE, or != conditions often defeat index usage. GORM translates these without warning, potentially causing full table scans on large tables for what appears to be a simple query.
PERF-211.original_description: Detect GORM Not conditions that may bypass database indexes
PERF-211.detection_notes: Match db.Not() or NOT IN/NOT LIKE patterns in db.Where or db.Raw call arguments on hot-path queries.

PERF-212.name: GORM Find Without Limit On Large Table
PERF-212.description: Calls db.Find(&records) without Limit() on a table that may contain many rows. This loads all rows into memory, potentially causing OOM for unbounded tables. Always paginate or limit queries on tables that can grow large.
PERF-212.original_description: Detect GORM Find without Limit on potentially large tables
PERF-212.detection_notes: Match db.Find(&slice) without a preceding .Limit() call; suppress for lookup tables known to be small.

PERF-101.name: HTTP Server Timeouts Not Configured
PERF-101.description: An http.Server is created without ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, or IdleTimeout configured. Without these timeouts, slow clients can hold connections indefinitely, exhausting goroutines and file descriptors under load. This is a Slowloris-style DoS vector in production Go services.
PERF-101.detection_notes: Match http.Server composite literals that do not set ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, or IdleTimeout fields.

PERF-102.name: HTTP Transport Not Shared Across Requests
PERF-102.description: Creates a new http.Transport instance per http.Client instead of sharing a single transport across clients. The http.Transport maintains a connection pool for keep-alive and TLS session resumption; creating new transports fragments this pool and causes redundant TLS handshakes.
PERF-102.original_description: http.Transport created per-client instead of shared globally
PERF-102.detection_notes: Match http.Transport{} composite literals; check if they are assigned to a globally shared variable or recreated per-client.

PERF-103.name: HTTP Response Body Not Closed
PERF-103.description: Failing to close http.Response.Body leaks TCP connections from the connection pool. Even if all bytes are read, the connection cannot be reused for keep-alive unless the body is fully consumed AND closed. Over time this causes connection pool exhaustion and file descriptor leaks.
PERF-103.detection_notes: Find calls to client.Do, client.Get, http.Get, http.Post where the response Body is not followed by defer resp.Body.Close() or explicit Body.Close() in the same function scope.

PERF-104.name: WriteHeader Called Multiple Times In Handler
PERF-104.description: Calls w.WriteHeader() multiple times in a single HTTP handler. The first call sets the status code; subsequent calls are silently ignored but still execute, wasting CPU on a no-op. This often indicates confused error-handling logic.
PERF-104.original_description: Multiple WriteHeader calls on the same ResponseWriter in one handler
PERF-104.detection_notes: Match two or more w.WriteHeader calls on the same http.ResponseWriter in a single handler function.

PERF-105.name: runtime.SetFinalizer On Hot Path Object
PERF-105.description: Uses runtime.SetFinalizer on objects created frequently in hot paths. Objects with finalizers require an extra GC cycle to collect. Each finalizer-eligible object is resurrected for the finalizer call, then collected on the next cycle, doubling GC work.
PERF-105.original_description: runtime.SetFinalizer on hot-path objects causing extra GC cycles
PERF-105.detection_notes: Match runtime.SetFinalizer calls reachable from request handlers or hot loops. Prefer explicit Close methods with finalizers only as safety nets.

PERF-106.name: sync.Map Used For Write-Heavy Workload
PERF-106.description: Uses sync.Map for workloads dominated by writes (Store) and updates. sync.Map is optimized for read-heavy, key-stable workloads with its internal read/dirty dual-map structure. Under frequent writes, a plain map with sync.Mutex outperforms sync.Map.
PERF-106.original_description: sync.Map in write-heavy patterns where plain map + mutex is faster
PERF-106.detection_notes: Match sync.Map declarations where Store/LoadAndDelete calls outnumber Load calls in the same scope.

PERF-107.name: encoding/binary Write Or Read Inside Loop
PERF-107.description: Uses encoding/binary.Write or binary.Read inside a loop. These functions use reflection to determine the size and layout of the target type on every call. For fixed-size types, manual BigEndian methods avoid reflection and are 10-20x faster.
PERF-107.original_description: binary.Read or binary.Write inside loop bodies where manual endian methods avoid reflection
PERF-107.detection_notes: Match binary.Read or binary.Write call expressions inside for/range loop bodies where the data parameter is a fixed-size basic type.

PERF-108.name: sort.Search Repeated In Loop
PERF-108.description: Calls sort.Search inside a loop with a function literal closure, allocating a new closure on every iteration. For repeated binary searches on the same sorted data, extract the predicate as a named function or pre-build a lookup structure.
PERF-108.original_description: sort.Search with closure inside loop bodies causing per-iteration allocation
PERF-108.detection_notes: Match sort.Search calls inside for/range loop bodies where the second argument is a function literal.

PERF-109.name: Map Key Recomputed In Loop Without Caching
PERF-109.description: Recomputes a map key inside a loop by calling an expensive function on every iteration when the key could be computed once and stored. Each recomputation adds CPU overhead proportional to the key computation cost times the loop count.
PERF-109.original_description: Repeated expensive map key computation inside loops without caching
PERF-109.detection_notes: Match fmt.Sprintf or custom function calls used as map index keys inside loop bodies where the arguments do not change across iterations.

PERF-110.name: sync.Pool Element Type Causes Allocation On Put
PERF-110.description: Stores non-pointer value types in sync.Pool (e.g., bytes.Buffer, not *bytes.Buffer). Each Get and Put operation boxes the value into interface{}, causing a heap allocation. Store pointer types instead to avoid the interface boxing allocation per pool operation.
PERF-110.original_description: sync.Pool storing non-pointer value types causing interface boxing allocations
PERF-110.detection_notes: Match sync.Pool New functions returning non-pointer struct types. Flag if New returns a value type rather than a pointer.

PERF-111.name: Range Over String Produces Rune Allocation
PERF-111.description: Converting a string to []rune(s) before ranging allocates a full rune slice on the heap. Ranging over the string directly with for _, r := range s decodes UTF-8 incrementally without allocating, cutting memory usage by 4x.
PERF-111.original_description: []rune conversion of strings before range loops where direct string range avoids allocation
PERF-111.detection_notes: Match RangeStmt where the range expression is a CallExpr to []rune with a string argument.

PERF-112.name: strings.ToLower Before Comparison Instead Of EqualFold
PERF-112.description: Compares strings with strings.ToLower(s1) == strings.ToLower(s2), allocating two intermediate strings even if they differ on the first byte. strings.EqualFold compares character-by-character without allocation and returns early on mismatch, up to 100x faster.
PERF-112.original_description: Case-insensitive string comparison using ToLower/ToUpper instead of EqualFold
PERF-112.detection_notes: Match BinaryExpr with == or != where both operands are calls to strings.ToLower or strings.ToUpper.

PERF-113.name: Single-Case Select Statement Instead Of Channel Op
PERF-113.description: Uses a select statement with a single case instead of a plain channel send or receive. The select adds goroutine scheduling overhead and a runtime.selectgo call. A direct channel operation compiles to fewer instructions.
PERF-113.original_description: Single-case select where direct channel send/receive would be simpler and faster
PERF-113.detection_notes: Match SelectStmt containing exactly one CommClause with no default branch.

PERF-114.name: Manual Loop Copy Instead Of copy() Builtin
PERF-114.description: Uses a for loop to copy elements from one slice to another index-by-index instead of the copy() builtin. copy() uses optimized memory routines (often memmove in assembly) that are 3-10x faster and handle memory overlap detection.
PERF-114.original_description: Manual for-loop slice copying where the copy builtin would be faster
PERF-114.detection_notes: Match ForStmt with := range assigning index i and value, body containing single assignment dst[i] = val or dst[i] = src[i].

PERF-115.name: strings.Compare Used For Equality Check
PERF-115.description: Uses strings.Compare(a, b) == 0 to check string equality. strings.Compare performs a full three-way comparison before the result is checked; a direct == comparison short-circuits at the first differing byte and avoids the function call overhead.
PERF-115.original_description: strings.Compare used for equality or ordering where direct comparison is cheaper
PERF-115.detection_notes: Match strings.Compare calls where the return value is compared to 0 with == or !=.

PERF-116.name: strings.Index Used For Contains Check
PERF-116.description: Uses strings.Index(s, sub) != -1 to check substring containment. strings.Contains(s, sub) communicates intent more clearly and has equivalent performance, but Index comparison patterns are often vestigial and obscure the boolean check.
PERF-116.original_description: strings.Index compared to -1 where strings.Contains would be clearer
PERF-116.detection_notes: Match strings.Index calls where the return value is compared to -1.

PERF-117.name: bytes.Compare Used For Equality Check
PERF-117.description: Uses bytes.Compare(a, b) == 0 to check byte slice equality. bytes.Equal(a, b) returns a boolean directly after the first difference, short-circuiting faster than a full three-way comparison.
PERF-117.original_description: bytes.Compare used for equality where bytes.Equal is faster and clearer
PERF-117.detection_notes: Match bytes.Compare calls where the return value is compared to 0 with == or !=.

PERF-118.name: Unnecessary http.NewRequest For Simple Methods
PERF-118.description: Uses http.NewRequest for simple GET/HEAD/POST requests when http.Get, http.Head, or http.Post would suffice. The convenience functions avoid manual request construction overhead when custom headers or body handling are not needed.
PERF-118.original_description: http.NewRequest for simple methods where http.Get/Post/Head wrappers are simpler
PERF-118.detection_notes: Match http.NewRequest with method GET, HEAD, or POST without custom headers or non-standard body handling.

PERF-119.name: Multiple Separate Appends Instead Of Spread Concatenation
PERF-119.description: Uses multiple sequential append calls to the same slice instead of a single variadic append. Each separate append may trigger independent capacity growth and reallocation; combining them into append(s, a, b, c) grows once if needed.
PERF-119.original_description: Consecutive append calls to the same slice that can be merged into one variadic call
PERF-119.detection_notes: Match 2+ sequential append calls to the same slice variable in the same block without intervening reads.

PERF-120.name: time.Now().Sub Instead Of time.Since
PERF-120.description: Uses time.Now().Sub(t) instead of time.Since(t). Both have identical behavior, but time.Since reads more naturally. When time.Now() has already been called in the function, using the cached value avoids a second syscall.
PERF-120.original_description: time.Now().Sub where time.Since is clearer and avoids duplicate syscall
PERF-120.detection_notes: Match time.Now().Sub called where time.Since provides identical behavior with clearer intent.

PERF-121.name: Struct Literal Instead Of Direct Type Conversion
PERF-121.description: Manually copies struct fields between types with identical underlying structures instead of using a direct type conversion T(v). The conversion is compile-time checked and zero-cost, while manual copying risks bugs from missed fields.
PERF-121.original_description: Manual struct field copying where direct type conversion is zero-cost
PERF-121.detection_notes: Match sequential AssignStmt nodes assigning fields from one struct to another with identical field types.

PERF-122.name: HasPrefix Followed By Slice Instead Of TrimPrefix
PERF-122.description: Checks strings.HasPrefix(s, prefix) and then slices s[len(prefix):] to extract the remainder. strings.TrimPrefix(s, prefix) returns the string with the prefix removed if present in a single call with clearer intent.
PERF-122.original_description: HasPrefix + manual slice instead of TrimPrefix
PERF-122.detection_notes: Match strings.HasPrefix followed by s[len(prefix):] slice expression in the same block.

PERF-123.name: Redundant make Argument With Zero Value
PERF-123.description: Calls make([]T, 0, cap) or make(map[K]V, 0) with an explicit zero length or capacity argument. The zero value is the default; omitting it produces identical behavior with less code.
PERF-123.original_description: make calls with explicit zero length or capacity arguments
PERF-123.detection_notes: Match make(T, 0) or make(T, 0, N) where the zero argument could be omitted.

PERF-124.name: strings.Replace With -1 Instead Of ReplaceAll
PERF-124.description: Uses strings.Replace(s, old, new, -1) with the magic number -1 to replace all occurrences. Since Go 1.12, strings.ReplaceAll(s, old, new) is available and communicates intent more clearly with identical performance.
PERF-124.original_description: strings.Replace with -1 where ReplaceAll is clearer
PERF-124.detection_notes: Match strings.Replace calls where the fourth argument is the integer literal -1.

PERF-125.name: Redundant nil Check Before append
PERF-125.description: Checks if a slice is nil before appending to it. append works correctly on nil slices (treating them as zero-length), so the nil check is unnecessary and adds a branch the compiler may not optimize away.
PERF-125.original_description: Nil checks on slices before append where append handles nil natively
PERF-125.detection_notes: Match if s != nil blocks whose body contains append(s, ...) or s = append(s, ...).

PERF-126.name: Redundant http.CanonicalHeaderKey Call
PERF-126.description: Calls http.CanonicalHeaderKey on a header key that is already in canonical form. Methods like Header.Get and Header.Set already canonicalize internally, making the explicit call redundant.
PERF-126.original_description: Redundant http.CanonicalHeaderKey calls on already-canonical keys
PERF-126.detection_notes: Match http.CanonicalHeaderKey applied to string literals already in canonical form or values already passed through http.Header methods.

PERF-127.name: Unnecessary fmt.Sprintf In Log Call
PERF-127.description: Wraps a static string in fmt.Sprintf before passing it to a log function. The log package Printf-style methods (log.Printf, log.Fatalf) handle formatting internally; wrapping in Sprintf first allocates an intermediate string.
PERF-127.original_description: fmt.Sprintf with static string passed directly to log functions
PERF-127.detection_notes: Match log.*(fmt.Sprintf("static string without format verbs")) patterns.

PERF-128.name: Multiple Independent Appends Can Be Combined
PERF-128.description: Performs append on a slice in multiple consecutive statements when all appended values are available at once. Each separate append may independently grow the backing array. Combining into a single variadic append reduces amortized growth overhead.
PERF-128.original_description: Consecutive independent append calls that can be merged
PERF-128.detection_notes: Match 3+ sequential append calls to the same slice variable without intervening reads or conditional logic.

PERF-129.name: Range Loop Copies Value When Only Index Needed
PERF-129.description: Ranges over a slice with for _, v := range and uses only the index or ignores v entirely. Even when the value is discarded, the runtime still copies each element. For large structs, the copy cost adds up. Use for i := range to iterate indices only.
PERF-129.original_description: Range loops that copy values when only the index is used
PERF-129.detection_notes: Match RangeStmt with key and value variables where the value variable is underscore or never read in the loop body.

PERF-130.name: Unnecessary Function Wrapper Adding Call Overhead
PERF-130.description: Wraps a function call in an anonymous function literal for no reason: func() { f(args) }(). This adds a closure allocation and indirect call overhead. If wrapping is needed for argument adaptation, use a minimal wrapper.
PERF-130.original_description: Unnecessary anonymous function wrappers that add call overhead
PERF-130.detection_notes: Match func() { someFunc(args) }() patterns where the wrapper provides no additional logic.

PERF-131.name: sync.Mutex Used Where sync/atomic Suffices
PERF-131.description: Uses sync.Mutex to protect a simple integer counter incremented in concurrent goroutines. atomic.AddInt64 is lock-free and significantly faster for simple arithmetic on integral types, avoiding lock/unlock overhead and goroutine blocking.
PERF-131.original_description: sync.Mutex guarding simple integer operations where atomic is lock-free
PERF-131.detection_notes: Match mu.Lock/mu.Unlock blocks where guarded operations are only simple integer increments, decrements, or reads.

PERF-132.name: Goroutine Spawned Without Context Propagation
PERF-132.description: Launches a goroutine that performs work (HTTP calls, DB queries) without receiving a context.Context from the parent. Without context propagation, the goroutine cannot be cancelled when the parent request times out, leading to wasted work and goroutine leaks.
PERF-132.original_description: Goroutines doing I/O work without context propagation from parent
PERF-132.detection_notes: Match go func() without ctx parameter where the body contains HTTP, DB, or other cancellable I/O operations.

PERF-133.name: sort.Slice Closure Allocation Inside Loop
PERF-133.description: Uses sort.Slice with a closure inside a loop or repeatedly-called function, causing a heap allocation for the closure on every call. For basic types, sort.Ints/sort.Strings/sort.Float64s avoid the closure allocation entirely.
PERF-133.original_description: sort.Slice with closure in hot paths where type-specific sort functions avoid allocation
PERF-133.detection_notes: Match sort.Slice inside loop bodies or request handlers with basic slice types.

PERF-134.name: Manual io.Read/Write Loop Instead Of io.Copy
PERF-134.description: Implements a manual read-write loop with io.Reader and io.Writer instead of using io.Copy. io.Copy uses an internal 32KB buffer and handles EOF, short reads, and errors correctly. Manual implementations often use suboptimal buffer sizes.
PERF-134.original_description: Manual read/write loops where io.Copy is both simpler and more efficient
PERF-134.detection_notes: Match for loops containing Read and Write calls in the body where the pattern matches io.Copy semantics.

PERF-135.name: encoding/gob Encoder Or Decoder Not Reused
PERF-135.description: Creates a new gob.NewEncoder or gob.NewDecoder for every message on a persistent connection instead of reusing one encoder/decoder pair per stream. gob encoders maintain internal type info caches and buffers; reusing avoids re-transmitting type definitions.
PERF-135.original_description: gob encoder/decoder created per message on persistent connections
PERF-135.detection_notes: Match gob.NewEncoder or gob.NewDecoder in functions containing for/range with net.Conn Read/Write patterns.

PERF-136.name: filepath.Join Repeatedly Called With Same Base
PERF-136.description: Calls filepath.Join(basePath, variant) repeatedly in a loop when the base path is constant across iterations. filepath.Join allocates a new string each call. Compute the joined path once by pre-joining with a placeholder or use direct concatenation.
PERF-136.original_description: filepath.Join with constant base path called repeatedly in loops
PERF-136.detection_notes: Match filepath.Join inside loop bodies where the first argument is a string literal or loop-invariant variable.

PERF-137.name: runtime.Caller Used In Hot Path
PERF-137.description: Calls runtime.Caller on hot paths to determine the calling function. runtime.Caller walks the stack, reading stack frames and program counter tables. On hot paths this adds measurable overhead; cache the result or use a compile-time constant.
PERF-137.original_description: runtime.Caller in request handlers or hot loops where it adds stack-walking overhead
PERF-137.detection_notes: Match runtime.Caller call expressions inside request handlers or for/range loop bodies.

PERF-138.name: runtime.Stack Used In Hot Path
PERF-138.description: Calls runtime.Stack on hot paths to capture goroutine stack traces. This allocates a buffer and formats the stack, which is expensive. Stack traces should be captured only for error reporting or debugging, not on production hot paths.
PERF-138.original_description: runtime.Stack in request handlers where stack trace formatting adds latency
PERF-138.detection_notes: Match runtime.Stack call expressions inside request handlers or production code paths.

PERF-139.name: Closure Allocates Due To Variable Escape
PERF-139.description: A closure captures multiple large outer variables, causing each to escape to the heap as separate allocations. Grouping captured variables into a single struct reduces N allocations to 1, since the struct itself escapes but fields are stored contiguously.
PERF-139.original_description: Closures capturing multiple large variables causing separate heap allocations
PERF-139.detection_notes: Match func() literals inside loops or hot functions referencing 2+ outer-scope variables of non-trivial size.

PERF-140.name: debug.SetGCPercent Misuse Or Tuning In Production
PERF-140.description: Calls debug.SetGCPercent with aggressive values (very low or -1 to disable) in production code without corresponding GOMEMLIMIT. Setting GCPercent too low increases GC frequency; setting to -1 disables GC entirely and risks OOM.
PERF-140.original_description: debug.SetGCPercent calls in production code without memory limit safeguards
PERF-140.detection_notes: Match debug.SetGCPercent call expressions; flag when value set below 50 or to -1 without nearby GOMEMLIMIT configuration.

PERF-141.name: URL.Query() Called Repeatedly Without Caching
PERF-141.description: Calls r.URL.Query() or r.URL.Query().Get(key) multiple times in a single handler. Each call to URL.Query() parses the raw query string and allocates a new url.Values map. Cache the result in a local variable to parse once.
PERF-141.original_description: Repeated calls to URL.Query() in the same handler without caching
PERF-141.detection_notes: Match 2+ r.URL.Query().Get or r.URL.Query() calls on the same *http.Request in one handler function.

PERF-142.name: http.MaxBytesReader Not Used For Untrusted Body
PERF-142.description: Reads from an HTTP request body (r.Body) without wrapping it in http.MaxBytesReader. A malicious client can send unbounded data, causing the server to allocate memory until OOM. MaxBytesReader limits the body to a configured maximum.
PERF-142.original_description: Request body reads without MaxBytesReader protection against unbounded input
PERF-142.detection_notes: Match io.ReadAll(r.Body) or json.NewDecoder(r.Body) without a preceding http.MaxBytesReader wrapper.

PERF-143.name: http.TimeoutHandler Not Used For Route-Level Timeouts
PERF-143.description: Exposes HTTP endpoints without per-route timeout enforcement via http.TimeoutHandler, relying solely on server-level timeouts. Per-route timeouts allow different deadlines for fast endpoints vs slow ones.
PERF-143.original_description: Route handlers without TimeoutHandler wrapping where per-route timeouts would improve resilience
PERF-143.detection_notes: Match mux.Handle or mux.HandleFunc calls without surrounding http.TimeoutHandler in the handler chain.

PERF-144.name: Content-Length Not Set In HTTP Response
PERF-144.description: Serves HTTP responses with known body sizes but does not set Content-Length header. Without Content-Length, Go uses chunked transfer encoding, which adds per-chunk overhead. Setting Content-Length enables client progress bars and connection reuse.
PERF-144.original_description: HTTP responses with known body size missing Content-Length header
PERF-144.detection_notes: Match w.Write calls in handlers where body size is known from a byte slice and Content-Length is not set before Write.

PERF-145.name: http.Request.WithContext Allocation On Hot Path
PERF-145.description: Calls r.WithContext(ctx) in middleware or request chains, which shallow-copies the entire http.Request struct (~2KB). Each WithContext call in the chain allocates a fresh copy. Prefer passing context values through other mechanisms.
PERF-145.original_description: r.WithContext called repeatedly in middleware chains causing per-request allocations
PERF-145.detection_notes: Match r.WithContext in functions named middleware or registered via Use/Group in multiple chain layers.

PERF-146.name: fmt.Sprintf With Single String And No Verbs
PERF-146.description: Uses fmt.Sprintf("%s", s) where s is already a string. This parses the format string, builds an argument list, and copies s to a new string. The fmt overhead is 20-50x more expensive than using s directly.
PERF-146.original_description: fmt.Sprintf with literal format containing only %s and a single string argument
PERF-146.detection_notes: Match fmt.Sprintf calls where the first argument is %s and there is exactly one additional argument of type string.

PERF-147.name: strings.Replace Call Where ReplaceAll Suffices
PERF-147.description: Uses strings.Replace with a negative count to replace all occurrences instead of strings.ReplaceAll. While performance is identical, the magic-number pattern obscures the 'replace all' intention and is a common source of bugs.
PERF-147.original_description: strings.Replace with negative count where ReplaceAll is cleaner
PERF-147.detection_notes: Match strings.Replace calls where the fourth argument is a negative integer literal.

PERF-148.name: Goroutine Leak Via Channel Send Without Guaranteed Receiver
PERF-148.description: Sends to an unbuffered channel from a goroutine without guaranteeing a corresponding receive on all code paths. If the receiver exits early via error return or context cancellation, the sender blocks forever on the unbuffered channel, leaking the goroutine.
PERF-148.original_description: Unbuffered channel sends in goroutines without guaranteed receive on all exit paths
PERF-148.detection_notes: Match ch <- value inside goroutine where the receive is guarded by select with default or conditional logic that may skip.

PERF-149.name: net.Conn Deadlines Not Set For Network Operations
PERF-149.description: Performs network I/O (Read, Write) on net.Conn without setting deadlines. Without deadlines, a stalled peer can block the goroutine indefinitely. SetReadDeadline and SetWriteDeadline ensure I/O operations time out rather than hanging.
PERF-149.original_description: Network Read/Write operations without configured deadlines
PERF-149.detection_notes: Match conn.Read or conn.Write calls where SetReadDeadline or SetWriteDeadline is not called on the same connection.

PERF-150.name: Large Stack Frame From Local Variables
PERF-150.description: Declares large arrays or many local variables that exceed the compiler stack frame threshold, preventing the function from being inlined. Functions that cannot be inlined add call overhead at every invocation.
PERF-150.original_description: Functions with large stack frames that prevent compiler inlining on hot paths
PERF-150.detection_notes: Find functions exceeding ~40 lines called from hot paths or loops.

PERF-151.name: Non-Inlinable Function On Hot Path Due To Complexity
PERF-151.description: A hot-path function exceeds the Go compiler inlining budget (~80 cost units as of Go 1.22). Each defer, for loop, and select adds to the budget. Functions that cross the threshold add ~5-15ns call overhead per invocation.
PERF-151.original_description: Hot-path functions exceeding compiler inlining budget due to complexity
PERF-151.detection_notes: Match functions >40 lines called from hot paths.

PERF-152.name: Header Copy Via Manual Loop Instead Of Clone
PERF-152.description: Copies HTTP headers by iterating over the source header map with a for-range loop and setting each key individually. http.Header.Clone() does a shallow copy in bulk and is significantly faster for header forwarding patterns.
PERF-152.original_description: Manual header copying loops where Header.Clone is more efficient
PERF-152.detection_notes: Match for k, v := range over Header variable with header.Add or header.Set in the loop body.

PERF-153.name: http.Cookie.String Called Repeatedly
PERF-153.description: Calls http.Cookie.String() multiple times for the same cookie in a request handler. The String method re-serializes the cookie each call. Cache the serialized string or extract only the needed field.
PERF-153.original_description: Repeated http.Cookie.String calls on the same cookie in one handler
PERF-153.detection_notes: Match cookie.String() called 2+ times on the same *http.Cookie variable in a single function scope.

PERF-154.name: Unnecessary http.HandlerFunc Type Conversion
PERF-154.description: Converts a function matching the http.HandlerFunc signature to http.HandlerFunc type and then passes it to http.Handle. Both Handle and HandleFunc accept compatible function types; the explicit conversion is a no-op.
PERF-154.detection_notes: Match http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { ... }) conversion patterns.

PERF-155.name: http.ServeMux Pattern Without Method Restriction
PERF-155.description: Registers route handlers that internally check r.Method with if/switch statements instead of using method-aware mux patterns or Go 1.22+ method routing. This duplicates HTTP method dispatching logic in every handler.
PERF-155.original_description: Handlers with inline HTTP method checks where mux-level method routing would work
PERF-155.detection_notes: Match if r.Method == ... or switch r.Method inside handler functions registered via Handle or HandleFunc.

PERF-156.name: Ranging Over String With Only Index Usage
PERF-156.description: Ranges over a string with for i, ch := range s when only the index i is used (ch is underscore). The runtime still decodes UTF-8 runes into ch on every iteration. Use a standard for i := 0; i < len(s); i++ loop to iterate bytes only.
PERF-156.original_description: String range loops decoding runes when only byte indices are needed
PERF-156.detection_notes: Match for i, _ := range str patterns and for _, r := range patterns where the rune value is unused.

PERF-157.name: Unnecessary Use Of fmt.Sprint With Single String
PERF-157.description: Calls fmt.Sprint(x) where x is already a string. fmt.Sprint parses the format, boxes arguments, and copies the result, returning x unchanged. The call is 100x+ overhead compared to using x directly.
PERF-157.original_description: fmt.Sprint or fmt.Sprintln called with a single string argument
PERF-157.detection_notes: Match fmt.Sprint or fmt.Sprintln calls with exactly one argument whose type is string.

PERF-158.name: Sorting Slice Of Basic Types With Closure
PERF-158.description: Uses sort.Slice with a closure to sort a slice of basic types (int, string, float64) instead of using sort.Ints, sort.Strings, or sort.Float64s. sort.Slice allocates a closure per call; the type-specific functions are closure-free.
PERF-158.original_description: sort.Slice on basic-type slices where type-specific sort functions avoid closure allocation
PERF-158.detection_notes: Match sort.Slice calls with []int, []string, []float64 where comparison is simple < or >.

PERF-159.name: Using json.NewDecoder Instead Of json.Unmarshal For Buffered Data
PERF-159.description: Uses io.ReadAll + json.Unmarshal to parse JSON from a reader, buffering the entire body before deserialization. json.NewDecoder(r).Decode(&v) streams directly from the reader, avoiding the intermediate byte slice allocation.
PERF-159.original_description: io.ReadAll + json.Unmarshal pattern where NewDecoder streaming is more efficient
PERF-159.detection_notes: Match io.ReadAll or ioutil.ReadAll immediately followed by json.Unmarshal on the same data.

PERF-160.name: sql.Open Inside Request Handler
PERF-160.description: Calls sql.Open inside a request handler or loop, creating a new connection pool per invocation instead of sharing a single *sql.DB singleton. Each sql.Open creates new connections, bypasses pool tuning, and leaks idle connections under load.
PERF-160.original_description: sql.Open called inside handlers or hot paths instead of once at startup
PERF-160.detection_notes: Match sql.Open call expressions inside functions with request-handler signatures or inside loop bodies.

PERF-161.name: rows.Err Not Checked After Iteration
PERF-161.description: Iterates over sql.Rows with for rows.Next() but never checks rows.Err() after the loop exits. This silently drops scan errors and row-level failures, potentially missing database errors that occurred mid-iteration.
PERF-161.original_description: rows.Next() loops missing rows.Err() check after the loop body
PERF-161.detection_notes: Match for rows.Next() patterns and verify rows.Err() is called after the closing brace of the loop.

PERF-162.name: db.Ping Inside Request Handler
PERF-162.description: Calls db.Ping or db.PingContext on every request to verify database liveness, adding an unnecessary round-trip per inbound call. Use a health-check endpoint or periodic goroutine with pings instead.
PERF-162.original_description: db.Ping inside handlers or middleware in request-serving hot paths
PERF-162.detection_notes: Match db.Ping or db.PingContext call expressions inside request-handler function bodies.

PERF-163.name: db.Query Instead Of QueryRow For Single Row
PERF-163.description: Uses db.Query or db.QueryContext for queries that return at most one row, then iterates with rows.Next() and rows.Scan(). db.QueryRow or db.QueryRowContext avoids the rowset iteration overhead for single-row queries.
PERF-163.original_description: db.Query followed by single rows.Next() where QueryRow suffices
PERF-163.detection_notes: Match db.Query expressions consumed in a block calling rows.Next() exactly once without a loop.

PERF-164.name: Missing Context In Database Calls
PERF-164.description: Uses database/sql methods like db.Query, db.Exec, or db.Prepare without their Context variants in request-serving code. The non-context variants lose cancellation propagation and request-level timeout enforcement.
PERF-164.original_description: db.Query/Exec/Prepare without Context suffix in request handlers where context is available
PERF-164.detection_notes: Match bare db.Query/Exec/Prepare calls inside functions with a ctx parameter where the Context variant is not used.

PERF-165.name: Not Implementing sql.Scanner For Custom Types
PERF-165.description: Scans database values into intermediate string or []byte variables and then parses them into a custom type, instead of implementing the sql.Scanner interface. The Scanner interface avoids the per-row intermediate allocation.
PERF-165.original_description: rows.Scan into temp variables followed by conversion where sql.Scanner avoids intermediate
PERF-165.detection_notes: Match rows.Scan into a primitive type followed by assignment or conversion to a custom type on the next line.

PERF-166.name: database/sql Null Handling Without sql.Null Types
PERF-166.description: Scans nullable database columns into pointer types (*string, *int) and dereferences them on every row. The sql.NullString, sql.NullInt64 types avoid heap-escaping and provide explicit Valid fields for null checking.
PERF-166.original_description: *string or *int used in rows.Scan where sql.Null types avoid per-row allocation
PERF-166.detection_notes: Match rows.Scan with pointer-type destination arguments where the pointer value is dereferenced for null checking.

PERF-167.name: WaitGroup.Add Inside Goroutine
PERF-167.description: Calls wg.Add(1) inside the goroutine body instead of before the go statement. This creates a race condition where wg.Wait() can return before Add is executed, causing zero or negative WaitGroup counters.
PERF-167.original_description: sync.WaitGroup.Add inside goroutine instead of before the go statement
PERF-167.detection_notes: Match wg.Add inside goroutine literal body; verify Add appeared before the enclosing go statement.

PERF-168.name: Large Struct Sent By Value Over Channel
PERF-168.description: Sends large structs by value through channels instead of using pointers. Each channel operation copies the full struct; for structs with many fields, strings, or slices, the copy cost can be significant under throughput.
PERF-168.original_description: Channel sends of large struct values where pointers would avoid copying
PERF-168.detection_notes: Match ch <- structValue where the struct type has more than ~3 fields or contains slice/map/string fields.

PERF-169.name: atomic.Value Frequent Store Allocation
PERF-169.description: Uses sync/atomic.Value for values frequently updated in hot paths. Each Store boxes the value into an interface{}, allocating on the heap. Typed atomic types (atomic.Int64, atomic.Pointer) or mutex are cheaper for frequent updates.
PERF-169.original_description: atomic.Value.Store called repeatedly in hot paths where typed atomics avoid interface boxing
PERF-169.detection_notes: Match atomic.Value declarations where .Store is called inside a loop or request handler.

PERF-170.name: sync.Once In Hot Function Path
PERF-170.description: Places sync.Once.Do() inside a function called on every request or in a loop, adding an atomic check overhead even after initialization completes. Move the sync.Once to package level so the hot path never touches it after the first call.
PERF-170.original_description: sync.Once.Do in request handlers where initialization belongs at startup
PERF-170.detection_notes: Match sync.Once.Do calls inside functions that are request handlers, middleware, or called within loop bodies.

PERF-171.name: Channel Used As Mutex
PERF-171.description: Uses a buffered channel with capacity 1 as mutual exclusion (send-to-acquire, receive-to-release) instead of sync.Mutex. Channel-based mutual exclusion adds goroutine scheduling overhead and is harder to make deadlock-free.
PERF-171.original_description: Channel used as mutual exclusion lock where sync.Mutex would be simpler and faster
PERF-171.detection_notes: Match make(chan struct{}, 1) or make(chan bool, 1) where send and receive are used for acquire/release with no data transfer.

PERF-172.name: WaitGroup.Wait Blocking Serving Goroutine
PERF-172.description: Calls sync.WaitGroup.Wait() in a request handler to wait for spawned goroutines, blocking the serving goroutine until all sub-tasks complete. Use a result channel or errgroup with context instead.
PERF-172.original_description: wg.Wait in request handlers blocking the serving goroutine
PERF-172.detection_notes: Match wg.Wait calls inside functions with request-handler signatures without worker pool delegation.

PERF-173.name: time.Tick Not Stopped Causing Goroutine Leak
PERF-173.description: Uses time.NewTicker in a long-running function without calling ticker.Stop(). The underlying timer goroutine continues firing after the ticker goes out of scope, leaking a goroutine. Always defer ticker.Stop().
PERF-173.original_description: time.NewTicker without corresponding Stop call when ticker leaves scope
PERF-173.detection_notes: Match time.NewTicker where Stop() is not called in any reachable code path before the ticker variable goes out of scope.

PERF-174.name: Closing Channel By Receiver
PERF-174.description: Closes a channel from the receiver side rather than the sender. If the sender tries to send after the close, it panics with 'send on closed channel'. The sender should own the channel lifecycle.
PERF-174.original_description: close(ch) where the enclosing goroutine primarily receives rather than sends
PERF-174.detection_notes: Match close(ch) inside a function that contains more <-ch receive operations than ch <- send operations.

PERF-175.name: Buffered Channel Spinning On Receive
PERF-175.description: Tight-loops on receiving from a buffered channel without a select or context deadline, consuming CPU when the channel is empty. A select with default + small sleep, or blocking receive with context cancellation, avoids CPU spin.
PERF-175.original_description: Tight-loop receives from buffered channels without backoff or cancellation
PERF-175.detection_notes: Match for { <-ch } or looped receives without select, context, or backoff mechanisms.

PERF-176.name: io.Copy Without Buffer Reuse
PERF-176.description: Uses io.Copy without providing a reusable buffer via io.CopyBuffer. io.Copy allocates a 32KB buffer internally on every call. When copying in a loop or per-request, providing a shared buffer avoids this repeated allocation.
PERF-176.original_description: io.Copy in hot paths without io.CopyBuffer with a pre-allocated reusable buffer
