PERF-176.detection_notes: Match io.Copy inside loop bodies or request handlers; check if io.CopyBuffer with a pooled buffer is used.

PERF-177.name: os.File.Readdir Instead Of os.ReadDir
PERF-177.description: Uses (*os.File).Readdir to list directory entries, which returns []os.FileInfo and stats every entry. os.ReadDir (Go 1.16+) returns []os.DirEntry, deferring the stat call and avoiding unnecessary filesystem syscalls.
PERF-177.original_description: os.File.Readdir where os.ReadDir avoids per-entry stat syscalls
PERF-177.detection_notes: Match .Readdir() method calls on *os.File values; suppress when FileInfo is explicitly required.

PERF-178.name: time.Format Instead Of time.AppendFormat
PERF-178.description: Uses time.Format which allocates a new string on every call. When formatting many timestamps in a loop, time.AppendFormat with a reusable byte slice avoids per-call string allocation, reducing GC pressure.
PERF-178.original_description: time.Format called repeatedly in loops where AppendFormat avoids allocation
PERF-178.detection_notes: Match t.Format(layout) inside loop bodies where the result is appended to a byte buffer.

PERF-179.name: strings.Replacer Not Used For Repeated Replace
PERF-179.description: Calls strings.Replace or strings.ReplaceAll repeatedly in a loop with the same old/new pairs on different inputs. strings.NewReplacer compiles the replacement set once and amortizes the cost across calls.
PERF-179.original_description: Repeated strings.Replace/ReplaceAll with same pairs in loops where NewReplacer is cheaper
PERF-179.detection_notes: Match strings.Replace or strings.ReplaceAll with string literal arguments inside loop bodies.

PERF-180.name: encoding/csv Reader Per Row
PERF-180.description: Creates a new csv.NewReader for each row or batch in a hot parsing path instead of reusing one reader instance. Each csv.NewReader allocates internal buffers; reusing the reader avoids repeated allocation.
PERF-180.original_description: csv.NewReader created per-row in loops where reuse avoids repeated allocation
PERF-180.detection_notes: Match csv.NewReader inside loop bodies or handler functions where the reader could be created once.

PERF-181.name: json.Decoder UseNumber Missing
PERF-181.description: Uses json.NewDecoder without calling .UseNumber() when decoded JSON contains numeric values that will be immediately used as integers. Without UseNumber(), the decoder parses all numbers as float64, requiring lossy float-to-int conversion.
PERF-181.original_description: json.NewDecoder without UseNumber where decoded struct has int fields
PERF-181.detection_notes: Match json.NewDecoder without following .UseNumber() when the decoded struct has int/int64 fields.

PERF-182.name: bufio.Writer Default Buffer Undersized
PERF-182.description: Uses bufio.NewWriter with the default 4096-byte buffer when typical writes are significantly larger. This causes excessive flush operations and small syscalls. Setting a buffer size matching expected write chunks with bufio.NewWriterSize reduces overhead.
PERF-182.original_description: bufio.NewWriter with default buffer when write sizes exceed 4096 bytes
PERF-182.detection_notes: Match bufio.NewWriter(w) without explicit size where subsequent Write calls pass large byte slices.

PERF-183.name: context.WithTimeout Inside Loop
PERF-183.description: Creates a new context.WithTimeout or context.WithCancel inside a loop body, allocating a new timer and context value chain on every iteration. Hoist the context derivation outside the loop or use a single long-lived context.
PERF-183.original_description: context.WithTimeout or context.WithCancel inside loop bodies
PERF-183.detection_notes: Match context.WithTimeout or context.WithCancel call expressions inside for/range loop bodies.

PERF-184.name: mime.TypeByExtension In Hot Path
PERF-184.description: Calls mime.TypeByExtension in request-serving paths to determine Content-Type headers. This function performs a map lookup and string manipulation per call. Cache the result or use a constant map for known MIME types.
PERF-184.original_description: mime.TypeByExtension called inside request handlers where caching avoids repeated lookup
PERF-184.detection_notes: Match mime.TypeByExtension call expressions inside handler or middleware functions.

PERF-185.name: http.DetectContentType In Request Handler
PERF-185.description: Uses http.DetectContentType in request-serving handlers to sniff file content types. This function reads up to 512 bytes and performs pattern matching on every call. Cache the detected type or use explicit MIME type mappings.
PERF-185.original_description: http.DetectContentType called inside request handlers or middleware
PERF-185.detection_notes: Match http.DetectContentType call expressions inside handler or middleware functions.

PERF-186.name: strings.Fields In Hot Parsing Path
PERF-186.description: Uses strings.Fields or strings.FieldsFunc in hot parsing paths, allocating a slice of all substrings at once. For large inputs, a bufio.Scanner or manual index-based parsing avoids allocating all fields simultaneously.
PERF-186.original_description: strings.Fields in hot parsing paths where streaming parsing reduces allocation
PERF-186.detection_notes: Match strings.Fields or strings.FieldsFunc inside loop bodies or handler functions on potentially large inputs.

PERF-187.name: template.HTMLEscaper In Hot Path
PERF-187.description: Calls template.HTMLEscaper or template.JSEscaper repeatedly in hot paths that build HTML or JavaScript fragments. These functions allocate a new string per call. Pre-escaping at startup or using html/template automatic escaping is more efficient.
PERF-187.original_description: template.HTMLEscaper or template.JSEscaper in loops or request-handler hot paths
PERF-187.detection_notes: Match template.HTMLEscaper or template.JSEscaper inside loop bodies or handler functions.

PERF-188.name: fmt.Sscanf In Hot Path
PERF-188.description: Uses fmt.Sscanf or fmt.Fscanf in performance-sensitive parsing paths. These functions use reflection to parse according to format strings and are orders of magnitude slower than direct strconv or manual parsing.
PERF-188.original_description: fmt.Sscanf or fmt.Fscanf inside loop bodies or request handlers
PERF-188.detection_notes: Match fmt.Sscanf or fmt.Fscanf with a known format string inside loop bodies or handler functions.

PERF-189.name: HTTP Response Body Not Drained Before Close
PERF-189.description: Calls resp.Body.Close() without fully consuming the response body first. HTTP/1.1 persistent connections cannot be reused unless the entire body is read. This causes new TCP connections and TLS handshakes for every request.
PERF-189.original_description: resp.Body.Close() without preceding body drain when response has unread data
PERF-189.detection_notes: Match defer resp.Body.Close() where resp is *http.Response; check if body is fully consumed before Close.

PERF-190.name: HTTP Client Missing Timeout
PERF-190.description: Creates an http.Client without setting the Timeout field, leaving outbound HTTP requests susceptible to hanging indefinitely. A hung request ties up a goroutine, and under load this cascade-exhausts goroutines and memory.
PERF-190.original_description: http.Client without Timeout field for outbound requests
PERF-190.detection_notes: Match http.Client composite literals that do not set the Timeout field.

PERF-191.name: Slice Of Pointers For Small Structs
PERF-191.description: Uses []*SmallStruct when []SmallStruct would provide better cache locality and avoid pointer-chasing overhead. Each pointer dereference causes a cache miss when iterating, and pointed-to values are individually heap-allocated and GC-scanned.
PERF-191.original_description: Slice of pointers to small structs where value slices improve cache locality
PERF-191.detection_notes: Match []*T declarations where T is a small struct and the slice is ranged in hot paths.

PERF-192.name: Map Without Size Hint
PERF-192.description: Creates a map with make(map[K]V) without a capacity hint when the approximate number of entries is known at allocation time. Without a hint, the map resizes its hash table multiple times as entries are added, causing rehashing and allocation churn.
PERF-192.original_description: make(map[K]V) without capacity hint when eventual size can be determined
PERF-192.detection_notes: Match make(map[K]V) without second argument where the map is populated from a slice with known length.

PERF-193.name: Not Resetting Timer In Loop
PERF-193.description: Creates a new time.NewTimer on every loop iteration instead of calling .Reset() on an existing timer. Even though Go 1.23+ improved Reset safety, creating a new timer per iteration allocates and registers a new runtime timer object.
PERF-193.original_description: time.NewTimer created inside loop where an existing timer could be Reset
PERF-193.detection_notes: Match time.NewTimer(d) inside for loop bodies; suppress when duration varies per iteration.

PERF-194.name: Using time.Sleep For Polling
PERF-194.description: Uses time.Sleep in a for loop as a polling mechanism instead of event-driven patterns like time.Ticker, fsnotify, or channel-based notifications. Sleep-based polling wastes CPU on wake-ups and adds latency equal to the sleep interval.
PERF-194.original_description: time.Sleep in loops used as polling where event-driven patterns avoid wake-up overhead
PERF-194.detection_notes: Match time.Sleep(d) inside an infinite or conditional for loop without select or channel wake-up operations.

PERF-195.name: log.Fatal Or log.Panic In Goroutine
PERF-195.description: Uses log.Fatal or log.Panic inside a goroutine that is not the main goroutine. log.Fatal calls os.Exit(1), terminating the entire process. log.Panic unwinds only the calling goroutine, leaving others in an undefined state.
PERF-195.original_description: log.Fatal or log.Panic inside goroutines launched from handlers or workers
PERF-195.detection_notes: Match log.Fatal, log.Fatalf, log.Fatalln, log.Panic inside go func() literal bodies or goroutine-launched functions.

PERF-196.name: JWT Token Parsing Per Handler
PERF-196.description: Parses and validates a JWT token in individual route handlers instead of in a shared authentication middleware. This duplicates cryptographic signature verification work on every handler in the chain.
PERF-196.original_description: JWT parsing called inside individual route handlers rather than in middleware
PERF-196.detection_notes: Match JWT Parse/ParseWithClaims calls inside route handler functions; suppress when middleware already validates.

PERF-197.name: Multiple io.ReadAll On Request Body
PERF-197.description: Reads the HTTP request body with io.ReadAll or json.NewDecoder in multiple places (middleware then handler). The body is a read-once stream; the second read gets EOF. Re-reading requires restoring via io.NopCloser with bytes.Buffer.
PERF-197.original_description: Multiple body reads on the same http.Request without restoration
PERF-197.detection_notes: Match 2+ body-read calls on the same *http.Request in the same function or across middleware-handler chains.

PERF-198.name: Content-Type Check With strings.Contains
PERF-198.description: Checks the Content-Type header using strings.Contains(header, "json") instead of a direct comparison or switch. strings.Contains performs a full substring scan and may false-match (e.g., application/vnd.api+json matches both 'json' and 'xml').
PERF-198.original_description: strings.Contains on Content-Type headers where precise comparison would be correct
PERF-198.detection_notes: Match strings.Contains on Content-Type header values where a precise check would be faster and correct.

PERF-199.name: Session Store Lookup Per Handler
PERF-199.description: Looks up the user session from a session store (Redis, database, cookie) on every individual route handler, instead of extracting it once in middleware. This multiplies network round-trips by the number of handlers in the chain.
PERF-199.original_description: Session store lookups inside individual route handlers rather than in middleware
PERF-199.detection_notes: Match session store Get/Start calls inside route handler functions.

PERF-200.name: Middleware Ordering Penalty
PERF-200.description: Places expensive middleware (authentication, body parsing, rate limiting) before cheap early-reject middleware (CORS preflight, request size check, IP allowlist). An early-rejected request still pays the cost of the expensive middleware.
PERF-200.original_description: Middleware chains where expensive work appears before cheap rejection middleware
PERF-200.detection_notes: Match router.Use or Group.Use call sequences; check if CORS/request-ID middleware appear after auth/body-parsing/rate-limiting.

PERF-201.name: CORS Preflight Handler Allocation
PERF-201.description: Implements a custom CORS preflight handler that allocates response headers or performs work for OPTIONS requests. Using a well-known CORS middleware that short-circuits on preflight is cheaper and well-tested.
PERF-201.original_description: Custom OPTIONS handlers with header manipulation where standard CORS middleware is cheaper
PERF-201.detection_notes: Match custom OPTIONS route handlers setting Access-Control-* headers; check if standard CORS middleware is imported.

PERF-202.name: json.Marshal Indent In Production Handler
PERF-202.description: Uses json.MarshalIndent or json.Encoder.SetIndent in production handlers to pretty-print JSON responses. Indentation adds whitespace (increasing response size) and requires extra allocation. Pretty-printing should be development-only.
PERF-202.original_description: json.MarshalIndent or SetIndent used in request handlers where compact output suffices
PERF-202.detection_notes: Match json.MarshalIndent or encoder.SetIndent inside handler functions or middleware.

PERF-203.name: net.IP.String Repeated In Hot Path
PERF-203.description: Calls net.IP.String() repeatedly on the same IP address in hot paths. Each call allocates a new string. Cache the string form once or use a formatting helper that writes to a reusable buffer.
PERF-203.original_description: Repeated net.IP.String calls on same IP in handlers where caching avoids allocation
PERF-203.detection_notes: Match ip.String() called 2+ times in the same function scope with the same IP variable.

PERF-204.name: GORM Updates With Map Without Select
PERF-204.description: Uses db.Model(&record).Updates(map[string]interface{}{...}) without a preceding .Select() clause. This updates all fields in the map unconditionally, including zero-value fields, generating larger UPDATE statements than needed.
PERF-204.original_description: GORM Updates with map argument without Select to restrict updated columns
PERF-204.detection_notes: Match db.Updates(map[...]) or db.Model().Updates(map[...]) without .Select preceding the call.

PERF-205.name: GORM Pagination Without Count Optimization
PERF-205.description: Calls db.Count(&total) followed by db.Offset(offset).Limit(limit).Find(&records) on every paginated list request. For large tables, OFFSET-based pagination degrades linearly; keyset pagination avoids this performance cliff.
PERF-205.original_description: GORM Count + Offset + Limit pattern where keyset pagination avoids OFFSET degradation
PERF-205.detection_notes: Match db.Count followed by db.Offset().Limit().Find in the same handler or function.

PERF-206.name: sqlx Unsafe Without Known Input
PERF-206.description: Uses sqlx.Unsafe to disable compile-time SQL name binding resolution when the query string is built from runtime input. Unsafe mode should only be used with static queries.
PERF-206.original_description: sqlx.Unsafe mode used with runtime-built queries where static queries should be used
PERF-206.detection_notes: Match sqlx.Unsafe or db.Unsafe() usage; check if the query argument is built from variables rather than a string literal.

PERF-207.name: Fiber ctx.SendFile Without Caching
PERF-207.description: Uses c.SendFile in Fiber handlers without setting cache headers (Cache-Control, ETag, Last-Modified). Each request re-reads and re-transmits the file, wasting disk I/O and network bandwidth for static assets.
PERF-207.original_description: Fiber SendFile without cache headers set for static assets
PERF-207.detection_notes: Match c.SendFile in Fiber route handlers without preceding cache header middleware.

PERF-208.name: Prometheus Counter Without Bounded Label Set
PERF-208.description: Creates a Prometheus counter or gauge vector with a label dimension that includes unbounded values (URL path with IDs, user emails). This causes unbounded time series growth, exhausting client memory and remote storage.
PERF-208.original_description: Prometheus metrics with unbounded label values from user input or IDs
PERF-208.detection_notes: Match NewCounterVec/NewHistogramVec with label names containing id, user, url, email, token.

PERF-209.name: Cobra PersistentPreRun In Every Command
PERF-209.description: Defines heavy PersistentPreRun or PersistentPostRun hooks on parent Cobra commands that execute for every subcommand invocation, even when the subcommand does not need the setup. Use subcommand-specific hooks or lazy initialization.
PERF-209.original_description: Expensive Cobra PersistentPreRun hooks running for every subcommand
PERF-209.detection_notes: Match PersistentPreRunE/PersistentPostRunE on parent commands; check hook body for heavy init or network calls.

PERF-210.name: go-redis KEYS Command In Application Code
PERF-210.description: Uses the Redis KEYS command via go-redis in application logic, which scans the entire keyspace and blocks the Redis server. For production, SCAN (rdb.Scan) iterates incrementally without blocking.
PERF-210.original_description: go-redis KEYS command in application handler paths where SCAN avoids server blocking
PERF-210.detection_notes: Match .Keys( call on go-redis client types inside handler, middleware, or application logic functions.

PERF-211.name: GORM Not In Select Clause
PERF-211.description: Uses GORM's Not() condition on indexed queries where NOT IN, NOT LIKE, or != conditions often defeat index usage. GORM translates these without warning, potentially causing full table scans on large tables.
PERF-211.original_description: GORM Not conditions that may bypass database indexes
PERF-211.detection_notes: Match db.Not() or NOT IN/NOT LIKE patterns in db.Where or db.Raw call arguments on hot-path queries.

PERF-212.name: GORM Find Without Limit On Large Table
PERF-212.description: Calls db.Find(&records) without Limit() on a table that may contain many rows. This loads all rows into memory, potentially causing OOM for unbounded tables. Always paginate or limit queries on tables that can grow large.
PERF-212.original_description: GORM Find without Limit on potentially large tables
PERF-212.detection_notes: Match db.Find(&slice) without a preceding .Limit() call; suppress for lookup tables known to be small.
